expiration_time.description: The time when the license bundle expires.

limit.description: A value of 0 or -1 corresponds to an unlimited license.

SpecEndorLicenseBundleInfo.description:
EndorLicenseBundleInfo provides information about a specific license
bundle.

SpecEndorLicenseBundleType.description:
Enum representing different types of Endor Labs license bundles.

 - ENDOR_LICENSE_BUNDLE_TYPE_UNSPECIFIED: Default value. Should not be used.
 - ENDOR_LICENSE_BUNDLE_TYPE_OSS_CORE: Open Source Software (OSS) Core license bundle.
 - ENDOR_LICENSE_BUNDLE_TYPE_OSS_PRO: Open Source Software (OSS) Pro license bundle.
 - ENDOR_LICENSE_BUNDLE_TYPE_CICD: Continuous Integration/Continuous Deployment (CI/CD) license bundle.
 - ENDOR_LICENSE_BUNDLE_TYPE_SECRETS: Secrets Management license bundle.
 - ENDOR_LICENSE_BUNDLE_TYPE_SBOM_HUB: Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) Hub license bundle.
 - ENDOR_LICENSE_BUNDLE_TYPE_SUPPLY_CHAIN: Supply Chain Security license bundle.
 - ENDOR_LICENSE_BUNDLE_TYPE_ENDOR_PATCHING: Endor Patching license bundle.
 - ENDOR_LICENSE_BUNDLE_TYPE_CODE: Discover risks in 1st party code.
 - ENDOR_LICENSE_BUNDLE_TYPE_ENDOR_OUTPOST: Endor Outpost license bundle.
 - ENDOR_LICENSE_BUNDLE_TYPE_SECURITY_REVIEW: Security Review license bundle.

SpecEndorLicenseFeatureType.description:
- ENDOR_LICENSE_FEATURE_TYPE_UNSPECIFIED: Type is undefined.
 - ENDOR_LICENSE_FEATURE_TYPE_SCM_POSTURE_MANAGEMENT: SCM Posture Management corresponds to endorctl's Github Scanner and
ensure your respository's configuration is correct.
 - ENDOR_LICENSE_FEATURE_TYPE_SCA: SCA corresponds to endorctl's Git Scanner and runs the Software
Composition Analysis.
 - ENDOR_LICENSE_FEATURE_TYPE_SECRETS: Secrets corresponds to endorctl's Secrets Scanner.
 - ENDOR_LICENSE_FEATURE_TYPE_SBOM_INGESTION: SBOM (Software Bill of Materials) Ingestion feature for ingesting SBOM
data.
 - ENDOR_LICENSE_FEATURE_TYPE_CONTAINER_SCAN: Container Scan feature for scanning container images.
 - ENDOR_LICENSE_FEATURE_TYPE_RECOMMENDATION: Recommendation feature provides recommendations based on change impact
analysis results.
 - ENDOR_LICENSE_FEATURE_TYPE_CICD_DISCOVERY: CI/CD Discovery feature discovers CI/CD pipelines tools and
configurations.
 - ENDOR_LICENSE_FEATURE_TYPE_ARTIFACT_SIGN: Artifact Signing feature allows signing artifacts.
 - ENDOR_LICENSE_FEATURE_TYPE_ENDOR_PATCHING: Endor Patching feature allows accessing Endor Labs factory.
 - ENDOR_LICENSE_FEATURE_TYPE_SAST: SAST feature allows scanning source code for security vulnerabilities.
 - ENDOR_LICENSE_FEATURE_TYPE_AI_MODELS: AI Models feature allows scanning source code for AI model usage.
 - ENDOR_LICENSE_FEATURE_TYPE_ENDOR_OUTPOST: Endor Outpost feature allows automated scanning on-premises.
 - ENDOR_LICENSE_FEATURE_TYPE_AI_INFERENCE: AI Inference feature allows querying AI models.
 - ENDOR_LICENSE_FEATURE_TYPE_SECURITY_REVIEW: Security Review feature allows reviewing security code.
 - ENDOR_LICENSE_FEATURE_TYPE_GITHUB_ACTIONS: Github Actions feature allows scanning Github Actions workflows.

limit.description: A value of 0 or -1 corresponds to an unlimited license.

expiration_time.description: The time when the license expires.

SpecEndorLicenseInfo.description:
EndorLicenseInfo provides information about a specific feature type and
its limit.

SpecEndorLicenseType.description:
EndorLicenseType represents the type of Endor Labs license.

 - ENDOR_LICENSE_TYPE_UNSPECIFIED: Type is undefined.
 - ENDOR_LICENSE_TYPE_FREE_TRIAL: FREE TRIAL license is used for self-service signup workflow.
 - ENDOR_LICENSE_TYPE_FREE_TIER: FREE TIER license for free restricted access to Endor Labs products.
 - ENDOR_LICENSE_TYPE_PREMIUM: PREMIUM license for paid customers.
 - ENDOR_LICENSE_TYPE_POV: Sales-led POC Customer.

config.description:
Configuration used by endorctl.
Contains everything except credential values.

The type of evaluation method.

 - EVALUATION_METHOD_TYPE_SYSTEM: SYSTEM indicates an evaluation method that is system wide.
 - EVALUATION_METHOD_TYPE_USER: USER indicates an evaluation method defined by a user.

- EXPORTER_TYPE_GHAS: EXPORTER_TYPE_GHAS indicates a GitHub Advanced Security exporter.
 - EXPORTER_TYPE_S3: EXPORTER_TYPE_S3 indicates an AWS S3 exporter.

line_patterns.description: List of patterns to match inside a file with a name that matches the file name pattern.

examples.description: List of examples of tool usage "in the wild".

uuid.description: Finding UUID.

name.description: Finding name.

approximation.description:
True if this finding is for an approximate dependency
based on the unresolved package dependencies.

create_time.description:
Time the finding was created.

Format: 2017-01-15T01:30:15.01Z
RFC 3339: https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc3339.txt.

target_dependency_package_name.description: Fully qualified name of the dependency. For example, eco://package@version.

SpecFindingData.description: Basic finding metadata that can be used to "plot trends".

SpecFindingLevel.description:
Finding severity level.

 - FINDING_LEVEL_CRITICAL: Critical finding.
 - FINDING_LEVEL_HIGH: Very important findings.
 - FINDING_LEVEL_MEDIUM: Important findings.
 - FINDING_LEVEL_LOW: Low priority finding.

processing_time.description: The total time in seconds that it took to process the request.

errors.description: Any errors encountered during the operation.

model.description: The model which will be used to generate the response of the AI request.

messages.description: The messages of the AI request.

tools.description: The tools of the AI request.

max_output_tokens.description:
MaxOutputTokens is the maximum number of tokens that the agent will
output.

SpecInput.description: Input message for the AI request.

SpecIssueType.description:
- ISSUE_TYPE_DIFF_TOO_LARGE: Files were skipped due to diff size limits.
 - ISSUE_TYPE_AI_ERROR: AI agent encountered errors but analysis continued.
 - ISSUE_TYPE_QUOTA_EXCEEDED: Quota for the namespace was exceeded.
 - ISSUE_TYPE_HIGH_DEMAND: High demand on our AI agents.

SpecJobPriority.description:
Priority definitions for the job.

 - JOB_PRIORITY_LOW: The job is low priority.
 - JOB_PRIORITY_MEDIUM: The job is medium priority.
 - JOB_PRIORITY_HIGH: The job is high priority.

SpecJobResponse.description: Response data returned by the job.

SpecJobState.description:
- JOB_STATE_NEW: The job was just created but never executed.
 - JOB_STATE_RUNNING: The job is running.
 - JOB_STATE_SUCCESS: The job has been successfully executed.
 - JOB_STATE_FAILED: The job has failed and will not be retried.
 - JOB_STATE_CANCELLED: The job has been cancelled/aborted by the service and will be retried.
 - JOB_STATE_RETRY: The job has failed but will be retried up to the max_retries limit.

SpecJobType.description:
JobType is used to match the job to its handler.
A new job type should be added when a new handler is added.

SpecLicenseConfiguration.description: LicenseConfiguration is the configuration for the Endor Labs license.

SpecLinterResultLevel.description:
The severity level of the linter result. Crafted using the Semgrep specification.

 - LINTER_RESULT_LEVEL_ERROR: ERROR indicates that a serious problem was found.
 - LINTER_RESULT_LEVEL_WARNING: WARNING indicates that a problem that is not considered serious was found.
 - LINTER_RESULT_LEVEL_INFO: INFO indicates that the notification is purely informational.
 - LINTER_RESULT_LEVEL_DEBUG: DEBUG indicates that this is a trace notification (debug).

SpecLinterResultOrigin.description:
The origin of the linter result.

 - LINTER_RESULT_ORIGIN_SEMGREP: The semgrep tool reported this result.
 - LINTER_RESULT_ORIGIN_SECRETS_SCANNER: The secrets scanner reported this result.
 - LINTER_RESULT_ORIGIN_SAST_SCANNER: The SAST scanner reported this result.
 - LINTER_RESULT_ORIGIN_LLM_SCANNER: The LLM scanner reported this result.
 - LINTER_RESULT_ORIGIN_AI_SAST_SCANNER: The AI SAST scanner reported this result.

args.description: Args is the arguments to pass to the server.

env_variables.description: Env variables that must be used.

MCPServerStdio is the configuration for an MCP server that is accessed
via a command line interface.

introduced.description:
The earliest version or commit in which malicious behaviour was
introduced.

fixed.description: The version or commit in which malicious behaviour was fixed.

osv_id.description: The OSV ID for the malware record.

version.description: The version of the malware record.

osv_id.description: The status of the malware record.

tool_calls.description:
The tool calls of the message.
This is only present if the role is ROLE_TOOLS.
This is the list of tools that the LLM needs to call.

time.description: The time is when the notification was last dismissed.

SpecNotificationState.description:
- NOTIFICATION_STATE_OPEN_NOTIFICATION_PENDING: New Notification has been created but notification is pending.
 - NOTIFICATION_STATE_OPEN: New Notification has been created and notification has been sent.
 - NOTIFICATION_STATE_DISMISSED: Notification has been dismissed by the user.
 - NOTIFICATION_STATE_SNOOZED: Notification has been snoozed by the user. Not used yet.
 - NOTIFICATION_STATE_RESOLVED_NOTIFICATION_PENDING: Notification has been resolved but notification is pending.
 - NOTIFICATION_STATE_RESOLVED: Notification has been resolved and the notification has been sent.
 - NOTIFICATION_STATE_DELETED: Notification has been marked for deletion.

chat_mode.description: ChatMode is the chat mode that the agent will use.

max_iterations.description:

that is isolated from the public internet and cannot be
used for training.
The code segments themselves are never stored anywhere,
only the embeddings are saved in the database.

clientBucket.description:
A Bucket of a conventional histogram, each of which is treated as
an individual counter-like time series by Prometheus.

offset.description: Gap to previous span, or starting point for 1st span (which can be negative).

clientBucketSpan.description:
A BucketSpan defines a number of consecutive buckets in a native
histogram with their offset. Logically, it would be more
straightforward to include the bucket counts in the Span. However,
the protobuf representation is more compact in the way the data is
structured here (with all the buckets in a single array separate
from the Spans).

bucket.description:
Buckets for the conventional histogram.

Ordered in increasing order of upper_bound, +Inf bucket is optional.

schema.description:
schema defines the bucket schema. Currently, valid numbers are -4 <= n <= 8.
They are all for base-2 bucket schemas, where 1 is a bucket boundary in each case, and
then each power of two is divided into 2^n logarithmic buckets.
Or in other words, each bucket boundary is the previous boundary times 2^(2^-n).
In the future, more bucket schemas may be added using numbers < -4 or > 8.

negative_span.description: Negative buckets for the native histogram.

negative_delta.description:
Use either "negative_delta" or "negative_count", the former for
regular histograms with integer counts, the latter for float
histograms.

Count delta of each bucket compared to previous one (or to zero for 1st bucket).

positive_span.description:
Positive buckets for the native histogram.
Use a no-op span (offset 0, length 0) for a native histogram without any
observations yet and with a zero_threshold of 0. Otherwise, it would be
indistinguishable from a classic histogram.

positive_delta.description:
Use either "positive_delta" or "positive_count", the former for
regular histograms with integer counts, the latter for float
histograms.

Count delta of each bucket compared to previous one (or to zero for 1st bucket).

exemplars.description: Only used for native histograms. These exemplars MUST have a timestamp.

clientMetricType.description:
- COUNTER: COUNTER must use the Metric field "counter".
 - GAUGE: GAUGE must use the Metric field "gauge".
 - SUMMARY: SUMMARY must use the Metric field "summary".
 - UNTYPED: UNTYPED must use the Metric field "untyped".
 - HISTOGRAM: HISTOGRAM must use the Metric field "histogram".
 - GAUGE_HISTOGRAM: GAUGE_HISTOGRAM must use the Metric field "histogram".

For Golang:

For Java:
Every artifact of a Java repository will be a package. For instance

The uuid of the parent project.

@type.description:
A URL/resource name that uniquely identifies the type of the serialized
protocol buffer message. This string must contain at least
one "/" character. The last segment of the URL's path must represent
the fully qualified name of the type (as in
`path/google.protobuf.Duration`). The name should be in a canonical form
(e.g., leading "." is not accepted).

In practice, teams usually precompile into the binary all types that they
expect it to use in the context of Any. However, for URLs which use the
scheme `http`, `https`, or no scheme, one can optionally set up a type
server that maps type URLs to message definitions as follows:

* If no scheme is provided, `https` is assumed.
* An HTTP GET on the URL must yield a [google.protobuf.Type][]
  value in binary format, or produce an error.
* Applications are allowed to cache lookup results based on the
  URL, or have them precompiled into a binary to avoid any
  lookup. Therefore, binary compatibility needs to be preserved
  on changes to types. (Use versioned type names to manage
  breaking changes.)

Note: this functionality is not currently available in the official
protobuf release, and it is not used for type URLs beginning with
type.googleapis.com. As of May 2023, there are no widely used type server
implementations and no plans to implement one.

Schemes other than `http`, `https` (or the empty scheme) might be
used with implementation specific semantics.

code.description:
The status code, which should be an enum value of
[google.rpc.Code][google.rpc.Code].

message.description:
A developer-facing error message, which should be in English. Any
user-facing error message should be localized and sent in the
[google.rpc.Status.details][google.rpc.Status.details] field, or localized
by the client.

details.description:
A list of messages that carry the error details.  There is a common set of
message types for APIs to use.

googlerpcStatus.description:
The `Status` type defines a logical error model that is suitable for
different programming environments, including REST APIs and RPC APIs. It is
used by [gRPC](https://github.com/grpc). Each `Status` message contains
three pieces of data: error code, error message, and error details.

You can find out more about this error model and how to work with it in the
[API Design Guide](https://cloud.google.com/apis/design/errors).

name.description: The name to give credit to.

The earliest version/commit where this vulnerability
was introduced in.

fixed.description: The version/commit that this vulnerability was fixed in.

name.description:
Required. Name of the package. Should match the name used in the package
ecosystem (e.g. the npm package name). For C/C++ projects integrated in
OSS-Fuzz, this is the name used for the integration.

ecosystem.description:

For the complete list of valid ecosystem names, see
<https://ossf.github.io/osv-schema/#affectedpackage-field>.

purl.description: Optional. The package URL for this package.

repo.description:
Required if type is GIT. The publicly accessible URL of the repo that can
be directly passed to clone commands.

id.description:
The `id` field is a unique identifier for the vulnerability entry. It is a
string of the format `<DB>-<ENTRYID>`, where `DB` names the database and
`ENTRYID` is in the format used by the database. For example:
“OSV-2020-111”, “CVE-2021-3114”, or “GHSA-vp9c-fpxx-744v”.

published.description: The RFC3339 timestamp indicating when this entry was published.

modified.description: The RFC3339 timestamp indicating when this entry was last modified.

withdrawn.description:
Optional. The RFC3339 timestamp indicating when this entry is considered to
be withdrawn.

aliases.description: Optional. IDs for the same vulnerability in other databases.

related.description:
Optional. List of IDs of closely related vulnerabilities, such as the same
problem in alternate ecosystems.

summary.description:
Required. One line human readable summary for the vulnerability. It is
recommended to keep this under 120 characters.

details.description: Required. Any additional human readable details for the vulnerability.

affected.description: Required. Affected commit ranges and versions.

references.description:
Optional. URLs to more information/advisories (including the
scheme e.g "https://").

database_specific.description:
Optional. JSON object holding additional information about the
vulnerability as defined by the database for which the record applies.

osvVulnerability.description:
A vulnerability entry.
The protobuf representation is *NOT* stable and only used for implementing
the JSON based API.

protobufNullValue.description:
`NullValue` is a singleton enumeration to represent the null value for the
`Value` type union.

The JSON representation for `NullValue` is JSON `null`.

 - NULL_VALUE: Null value.

 - CONFIDENCE_LEVEL_LOW: Low priority finding.

agent_contexts can include multiple instances of AgentContext. The context

If true, the session will be visible to all users in the same namespace.

A slice of stop words that are allowed to be ignored.
This targets the _secret_, not the content of the regex match like the
regexes slice.

v1Allowlist.description: Allows a rule to be ignored for specific regexes, paths, and/or commits.

v1ArtifactOperationCommand.description:
- ARTIFACT_OPERATION_COMMAND_SIGN: ARTIFACT_OPERATION_COMMAND_SIGN is used to sign an artifact.
 - ARTIFACT_OPERATION_COMMAND_VERIFY: ARTIFACT_OPERATION_COMMAND_VERIFY is used to verify an artifact.
 - ARTIFACT_OPERATION_COMMAND_REVOKE_SIGNATURE: ARTIFACT_OPERATION_COMMAND_REVOKE_SIGNATURE is used to revoke an artifact signature.

v1ArtifactOperationResult.description:
The result of an artifact operation.

 - ARTIFACT_OPERATION_RESULT_UNSPECIFIED: No validator.
 - ARTIFACT_OPERATION_RESULT_SUCCESS: Success in the artifact operation.
 - ARTIFACT_OPERATION_RESULT_NO_ARTIFACT: Couldn't find any signatures for the given artifact name.
 - ARTIFACT_OPERATION_RESULT_NO_SIGNATURE: No signature found for the given digest of the artifact.
 - ARTIFACT_OPERATION_RESULT_TIME_MISMATCH: The timestamp associated with the signature is outside the
time window of the validity of the certificate.
 - ARTIFACT_OPERATION_RESULT_INVALID_SIGNATURE: The stored signature is invalid.
 - ARTIFACT_OPERATION_RESULT_UNKNOWN_CA: Unable to verify with a known CA.
 - ARTIFACT_OPERATION_RESULT_REVOKED_SIGNATURE: Signature has been revoked.
 - ARTIFACT_OPERATION_RESULT_CLAIMS_MISMATCH: Claims/attestations in the signature don't match input.
 - ARTIFACT_OPERATION_RESULT_BAD_CERTIFICATE: Empty or corrupted certificate.
 - ARTIFACT_OPERATION_RESULT_INVALID_KEY_TYPE: Could not find the right type of key in the certificate.
 - ARTIFACT_OPERATION_RESULT_CORRUPTED_SIGNATURE: Could not decode signature.
 - ARTIFACT_OPERATION_RESULT_KEY_GEN_FAILURE: Failed to generate private key.
 - ARTIFACT_OPERATION_RESULT_CSR_GEN_FAILURE: Failed to generate certificate request.
 - ARTIFACT_OPERATION_RESULT_SIGN_FAILURE: Failed to sign.
 - ARTIFACT_OPERATION_RESULT_INVALID_ARTIFACT_NAME: Invalid artifact name.

artifact_name.description: The name of the artifact being signed or verified.

digest.description:
Used only for the REVOKE command and is the source repository digest for the
signature to be revoked. The artifact name has to be provided as well.

uuid.description: The UUID of an artifact signature object.

certificate.description: The certificate obtained for this sign operation.

signature.description: The signature of the build artifact.

timestamp.description: The timestamp of the signing event.

revoked.description: Set to true if the signature gets revoked.

source_repository_ref.description:
Source repository ref. This also exists in the certificate but it's also here
to quickly look up entries that match.

uuid.description: The UUID of the assured package version.

v1AssuredPackageVersion.description:
AssuredPackageVersion is a  package version that have been built, tested, and patched by Endor Labs.
It includes details on how the version was built, tested, and patched, as well as information on the vulnerabilities that have been addressed through the patches.

package_name.description:
The name of the package of this assured package version. It is
calculated automatically from the assured package version name.

sha256.description:
SHA-256 of the assured package version.
For Java, this is the SHA of the jar file.

reproducible_build_source_code_url.description:
The URL to download the source code
to reproduce the build of the package version.
This is a signed URL valid only for four hours.

uuid.description: The UUID of the audit log.

v1AuditLog.description: AuditLog represents a CRUD operation on a message.

message_uuid.description: Represents the UUID of the resource which is accessed.

claims.description:
Claims in the context.

For example:

  "claims": [
    "ID=19946263398469405566117368291178288812",
    "email=scheduler",
    "email=scheduler@endor.ai",
    "user=scheduler@endor.ai@x509",
    "user=scheduler@x509",
    "x509",
    "issuer=https://api.endorlabs.com/v1"
  ]

key.description: The identifier of an API key.

secret.description: The secret for the specified API key.

enable_managed_identity.description: Auth using Azure managed identity for system.

managed_identity_client_id.description: The client ID of the Azure managed identity.

uuid.description: The UUID of the authentication log.

v1AuthenticationLog.description: Represents a CRUD operation on a message.

success.description: Success is set to true if the authentication is succesful.

authorized_tenants.description: The tenants accessible by this user at the time of authentication.

error_message.description: Error message details if the authentication fails.

status.description: The return code of the authentication.

claims.description:
Claims in the context.

For example:

  "claims": [
    "ID=19946263398469405566117368291178288812",
    "email=scheduler",
    "email=scheduler@endor.ai",
    "user=scheduler@endor.ai@x509",
    "user=scheduler@x509",
    "x509",
    "issuer=https://api.endorlabs.com/v1"
  ]

remote_address.description: The remote IP address that was discovered.

v1AuthenticationResponse.description: Response to the request includes a token and the token expiration time for an authenticated user.

uuid.description: The UUID of the authorization request.

claims.description: Establishes the list of claims for attribute based access control.

target_namespace.description:
The target namespace of the request. If empty, the request is for a global object
that doesn't have a namespace.

v1Authorization.description: Represents an authorization request in the system.

uuid.description: The UUID of the AuthorizationPolicy resource.

propagate.description: Indicates that the object should be visible in the child namespaces.

v1AuthorizationPolicy.description: Represents an authorization policy in the system.

clause.description:
The list of claims that must match. This is essentially
an AND operator. User claims must match all the
criteria in the clause for the policy to match.

For example, ["group=X", "email=user@endor.ai"] will match
users of group X with this email.

target_namespaces.description:
The target namespaces of the policy. The policy can
apply to one or more target namespaces. The targets
can only be the current namespace or its children.

propagate.description:
Propagate indicates that the policy should include
the children of the target namespace as well as allowed targets.

expiration_time.description:
Disables this policy after its expiration time.
If expiration time is not provided, the policy will never expire.

is_support_policy.description:
is_support_policy indicates that the policy is a support policy and
cannot be altered without using the SupportAccess API.

v1AutoDetectResult.description: Message for an auto detect result.

full_pr_scan.description: full_pr_scan indicates whether a full scan should be enabled during PRs.

full_push_scan.description:
full_push_scan indicates whether a full scan should be enabled during
pushes.

included_paths.description: included_paths is a list of paths that should be included in the scan.

excluded_paths.description: excluded_paths is a list of paths that should be excluded from the scan.

languages.description:
languages is a list of languages that should be scanned. If emtpy
default values are used.

call_graph_languages.description:
call_graph_languages is the list of languages that should be used to
calculate call graphs. If empty, default values are used.

additional_environment_variables.description:
additional_environment_variables is a list of additional environment
variables that must be used, specific to languages or tools.

enable_automated_pr_scans.description:
enable_automated_pr_scans for this repository will allow the github
app to selectively scan PRs and merges for this repository.
If the project is not part of a github app, this setting will be ingored.

disable_code_storage.description:
disable_code_storage is a boolean value that indicates if the source code related to a finding
should be stored or displayed. Default will be off.

disable_code_snippet_storage.description:
disable_code_snippet_storage is a boolean value that indicates if the source code snippet
related to a finding should be stored or displayed. Default will be off.

v1AutomatedScanParameters.description:
AutomatedScanParameters represents the parameters that must be applied during
automated cloud scans to the projects associated with the corresponding
profile.

v1AutomatedScanParametersConfig.description: Message for automated scan parameter configuration.

personal_access_token.description: PAT of the user doing integration to fetch resources using REST APIs.

v1BatchDependencyMetadata.description: Request to create multiple DependencyMetadata objects.

v1BatchFileSegments.description: Request used to create or delete multiple file segment objects.

access_token.title:
The access token associated with the Bitbucket Cloud account.
https://support.atlassian.com/bitbucket-cloud/docs/access-tokens

host_url.description: The URL of the workspace (only for cloud) or project.

http_access_token.description: The HTTP Access Token used for Bitbucket Datacenter authentication.

dependency_graph.title:
A dependency graph is a K/V pair of package names.
The format of the key must be:
- mvn://MVN_CENTRAL/foo:bar@2.1
- mvn://CLIENT_REPO/foo:bar@2.1-SNAPSHOT
- go://github.com/myorg/myrepo/mypkg@sha

dependencies.description:
Different package managers use different dependency graph serialization
strategies. Include the serialized list here to avoid re-implementing
this within endoctl.

dependency_files.description:
The list of the dependency files used to create the BOM.
This field is optional and is only set when the source code of the
package versions is known. It will contain the list of relative path of
files used to compute the BOM. For instance for Golang, it will contain
the go.mod and go.sum.

allow_create.description: Anyone with write access to the repository can create a new branch.

allow_delete.description: Anyone with write access to the repository can delete a branch.

allow_force_push.description: Anyone with write access to the repository can force push to a branch.

allow_fork_sync.description: Users can pull changes from upstream when the branch is locked.

allow_review_bypass.description: Specific users, teams, or apps can bypass pull request requirements.

allow_self_approval.description: Users can approve their own pull requests.

dismiss_stale_approvals.description: Approving reviews are automatically dismissed when someone pushes a new commit.

enforce_admins.description: All configured restrictions are also enforced for administrators.

linear_history.description: Linear commit Git history is enforced, which prevents anyone from pushing merge commits to a branch.

require_code_owner_approval.description: Pull request merges are blocked until code owners have reviewed.

require_comment_resolution.description: All pull request comments must be resolved before it can be merged.

require_status_checks.description: All status checks must pass before merging.

require_update.description: Branch must be up to date before merging.

restrict_dismiss.description: Specific users, teams, and apps can dismiss pull request reviews.

restrict_push.description: Specific users, teams, and apps can push code.

num_approvals_required.description: A minimum number of approvals is required to merge a pull request.

v1BranchAccess.description: BranchAccess contains normalized branch protection settings.

v1BranchProtection.description: The list of branch protection rules for protected branches.

uuid.description: The UUID of a CI run.

v1CIRun.description: Represents the information captured from a CI run of endorctl.

start_time.description: Start time is the time a CI run started.

end_time.description: End time is the time a CI run ended.

run_tags.description: The tags associated with this CI run.

baseline.description: Baseline repository version for this CI run.

v1CallGraphAnalysisType.description:
Call graph analysis type for findings.

 - CALL_GRAPH_ANALYSIS_TYPE_UNSPECIFIED: Unspecified call graph analysis type.
 - CALL_GRAPH_ANALYSIS_TYPE_FULL: Full call graph analysis was performed.
 - CALL_GRAPH_ANALYSIS_TYPE_PRECOMPUTED: Precomputed call graph analysis was used.

uuid.description: The UUID for a call graph resource.

zstd_bytes.description:
Callgraph proto bytes are encoded in zstd bytes.
The caller is responsible to compress/uncompress the bytes.

related_object.description: An internal value that identifies the callgraph storage location.

storage_url.description: The URL of the storage location of the callgraph.

v1CallGraphData.description: CallGraphData represents a call graph data request in the system.

priority.description:
The priority of Cargo.
Priorities are relative. If multiple package managers
have the same priority, the order between them can be random.
Lower number means higher priority.

url.description: The url of the crates registry that Cargo must use.

token.description: A token that must be provided by the repository owner.

v1CargoSpec.description: CargoSpec represents a configuration for Cargo.

centered_score.description:
Centered score for the category. This score is
normalized to be centered on 5 and is a float, so it has a higher resolution.

raw_score.description:
Raw score for the category. This is the score by
adding up the score factors, not centered around 5.

v1CategoryScore.description:
The score for one score category. The is the overall score
for a specific category. It is computed from the score factors that
have been created for the category. There is going to be only a single
instance of this information for the repo per score category.

Example:

Category: SCORE_CATEGORY_BEST_PRACTICES

Score: 5

Description: This score provides an overview of how this repo does when considering security best practices.

The value is a number in the range 1 to 10, with 10 being the best.

runner_environment.description: Specifies whether the build took place in platform-hosted cloud infrastructure or customer/self-hosted infrastructure.

source_repository.description: Source repository on which the build was based.

source_repository_digest.description: Immutable reference to a specific version of the source code on which the build was based.

source_repository_owner.description: Source repository owner of the source repository on which the build was based.

source_repository_ref.description: Source Repository Ref on which the build run was based.

build_config_name.description: Name of the top-level/initiating build instructions (workflow).

build_config_digest.description: Immutable reference to the specific version of the top-level/initiating build instructions (workflow SHA).

certificate_identity.description: Immutable reference to the specific identity expected in a valid certificate. Valid values include email address, DNS names, IP addresses, and URIs.

certificate_oidc_issuer.description: Immutable reference to the specific OIDC issuer expected in a valid certificate. For example, https://token.actions.githubusercontent.com or https://oauth2.sigstore.dev/auth.

v1CertificateExtensions.description: CertificateExtensions contains the certificate extensions retrieved from an identity token.

uuid.description: The UUID of a certificate request.

v1CertificateRequest.description: CertificateRequest represents a certificate signing request.

value.description: A string representation (base64 encoded) of the SHA/MD5 checksum of the package.

v1Checksum.description: The details of checksum including the algorithm used to prepare the checksum.

name.description: Tool name.

categories.description: List of control function categories (based on static name to category mapping).

evidence.description: Details of where and/or how the tool was discovered.

v1CiCdTools.description: CiCdTools Metric Value contains information about CI/CD tools discovered in the repository version.

v1CisCategory.description:
CisCategory represents a CIS benchmark requirement category per
CIS Software Supply Chain Security - Base v1.0.0
https://workbench.cisecurity.org/benchmarks/7555.

v1CisReqId.description:
CisReqId represents a CIS benchmark requirement per
CIS Software Supply Chain Security - Base v1.0.0
https://workbench.cisecurity.org/benchmarks/7555.

v1CisScorecard.description:
CisScorecard represents the CIS benchmark scorecard for the parent repository.
A scorecard contains a list of benchmarks.

v1CisSubCategory.description:
CisSubCategory represents a CIS benchmark requirement sub-category per
CIS Software Supply Chain Security - Base v1.0.0
https://workbench.cisecurity.org/benchmarks/7555.

name.description: Fully qualified name of the class.

v1Class.description:
Contains metadata information about the source code for a specific Java
class.

uuid.description: The UUID of the client log.

v1ClientLog.description: ClientLog represents a CRUD operation on a message.

json_payload.description: The json payload of the message.

tags.description: The tags that are associated with the log message.

timestamp.description: The timestamp of the log message.

log_messages.description: An array of log messages provided as JSON payloads.

source.description: The source that is creating this message.

priority.description:
The priority of the Cocoapod package manager.
Priorities are relative. If multiple package managers
have the same priority, the order between them can be random.
Lower number means higher priority.

url.description: The URL of the registry that the Cocoapods package manager must use.

v1CocoapodSpec.description:
Represents a configuration for the Cocoapod package manager.
Cocoapods does not support any registry authentication.

v1CodeOwnerData.description: Code owner information for a file path or pattern.

uuid.description: The UUID of the object.

v1CodeOwners.description: Code owner information for a project.

patterns.description:
Map of code owner data indexed by file path or pattern.

The list of owners is refreshed based on the content of
the CODEOWNERS file from the default repository version,
if present. Other fields, such as the list of labels,
can be populated manually as needed.
For projects that do not use a standard CODEOWNERS file the
entire map can be populated manually.

For more information please see the GitHub CODEOWNERS syntax.

Note:
If there is one owner for files under e.g. /src/typescript/ and
a different owner for all *.js files, then all js files under
src/typescript/ are mapped to both owners instead of basing it
on the order of the patterns in the CODEOWNERS file where the
last matching pattern takes the most precedence.

v1ColumnTypes.description:
The supported data types.

 - COLUMN_TYPES_UNSPECIFIED: EMPTY
 - COLUMN_TYPES_INT32: INT32
 - COLUMN_TYPES_STRING: String
 - COLUMN_TYPES_FLOAT: Float
 - COLUMN_TYPES_BOOL: Bool
 - COLUMN_TYPES_STRING_LIST: List of strings

v1ColumnValue.description:
The container for various data types that can be contained in a column.
The information from column_types in TableDescription is used to read the values.

current.description: The current version of the direct dependency being upgraded.

upgrade.description: The new version of the direct dependency being upgraded.

conflicting_current.description: The conflicting transitive dependency, current version.

conflicting_upgrade.description: The conflicting transitive dependency, version after the upgrade.

conflicting_directs_current.description: The direct dependencies using the conflicting transitive dependency in the current version.

conflicting_directs_upgrade.description: The direct dependencies using the conflicting transitive dependency after the upgrade.

v1ContainerArchitecture.description:
Container architecture.

 - CONTAINER_ARCHITECTURE_UNSPECIFIED: Unspecified architecture.
 - CONTAINER_ARCHITECTURE_UNKNOWN: Unknown architecture.
 - CONTAINER_ARCHITECTURE_AMD64: amd64 (64-bit x86) architecture.
 - CONTAINER_ARCHITECTURE_386: 386 (32-bit x86) architecture.
 - CONTAINER_ARCHITECTURE_ARM: arm (32-bit ARM) architecture.
 - CONTAINER_ARCHITECTURE_ARM64: arm64 (64-bit ARM) architecture.
 - CONTAINER_ARCHITECTURE_PPC64LE: ppc64le (PowerPC 64-bit, little-endian) architecture.
 - CONTAINER_ARCHITECTURE_PPC64: ppc64 (PowerPC 64-bit, big-endian) architecture  .
 - CONTAINER_ARCHITECTURE_MIPS64LE: mips64le (MIPS 64-bit, little-endian) architecture.
 - CONTAINER_ARCHITECTURE_MIPS64: mips64 (MIPS 64-bit, big-endian) architecture.
 - CONTAINER_ARCHITECTURE_MIPSLE: mipsle (MIPS 32-bit, little-endian) architecture.
 - CONTAINER_ARCHITECTURE_MIPS: mips (MIPS 32-bit, big-endian) architecture.
 - CONTAINER_ARCHITECTURE_S390X: s390x (IBM System z 64-bit, big-endian) architecture.
 - CONTAINER_ARCHITECTURE_WASM: wasm (WebAssembly 32-bit) architecture.

name.description: The name of the base image. For example, "debian:bookworm-slim".

v1ContainerBaseImage.description: The base image of a container Image.

digest.description:
The SHA256 digest of the layer,
where a dependency is found to be present in the container.

file_locations.description:
The locations of the files in the container,
through which the dependency is evident to be present in the container.

v1ContainerDependencyLayer.description: ContainerDependency is a dependency of a container image.

layers.description:
Layers of the container image where a dependency is found.
This is an optional field.
It is only set when target dependency is found via container image scan.

digest.description: digest is the sha256 digest of the layer.

command.description:
The command that was run to create the layer.
For example, `COPY /app /app`.
This is an optional field.

base_layer.description: True if the layer is came through the base image otherwise false.

v1ContainerLayer.description: A layer of a container image.

digest.description: The SHA256 digest of the container image layer.

file_locations.description: File locations on the container file system that reference the dependency.

base_layer.description: Set to true if this is the base image layer.

digest.description: The SHA256 digest of the container image.

layers.description:
The list of layers of the container in the order they are applied to
assemble the container image. The layers are the SHA256 digest of the
container image layers. The list is equivalent to the output of `docker
inspect --format='{{.RootFS.Layers}}' <image>`.

distribution.description: The OS distribution of the base image.

version.description: The version of the OS distribution of the base image.

working_directory.description: The working directory of the container.

v1ContainerMetadata.description: ContainerMetadata is the metadata of a container image.

will_be_deleted_at.description:
Time that all objects in this context will be deleted.
This field is deprecated and will be removed in the future.
Please use the meta.will_be_deleted_at field instead.

id.description: The context ID, such as a pull request ID or branch reference.

tags.description:
A list of tags applied to a context. Used primarily for CI and SBOM
contexts.

v1Context.description: Contexts keep objects from different scans separated.

v1Correctness.description:
Correctness represents a correctness assessment.

 - CORRECTNESS_TRUE_POSITIVE: The AI analysis result is a true positive.
 - CORRECTNESS_FALSE_POSITIVE: The AI analysis result is a false positive.
 - CORRECTNESS_FALSE_NEGATIVE: The AI analysis result is a false negative.
 - CORRECTNESS_UNKNOWN: The AI analysis result is unknown.

v1CorrectnessAnalyzer.description: CorrectnessAnalyzer represents the analyzer used to determine the correctness of a linter result.

count.description: Number of objects matching the given list parameters.

v1CountResponse.description: Response to a list count request.

v1CustomTemplate.description: CustomTemplate is the type specific custom template.

uuid.description: The UUID of the dashboard configuration object.

propagate.description: Propagate indicates that the object should be visible in child namespaces.

v1DashboardConfig.description: DashboardConfig represents the dashboard configuration for a namespace.

action.description: Action to be executed with delete request.

force.description:
Force will force the deletion of the resource if any
checks fail.

uuid.description: UUID is a universally unique identifier for a namespace resource.

v1Demo.description:
Demo provides a mechanism for isolating groups of resources.
Demos may be defined in a parent child hierarchy.

workflow_url.description:
The link to the GitHub actions workflow.
Only the latest run is included.

demo_projects.description: The list of available projects that a user can select.

requested_project.description: The project that must be scanned.

initiate_scan.description: Initiates the scan with an update operation.

code_url.description: The link to the corresponding tag in Github.

workflow_id.description: The GitHub ID of the workflow.

uuid.description: The UUID of the object.

v1DependencyMetadata.description:
Information about the relationship between a root package version (importer)
and one of its dependencies.
DependencyMetadata objects are children of the root PackageVersion object and
belong to the same project and namespace as the root PackageVersion object.
They are connected to the importer project through
spec.importer_data.project_uuid, and to the dependency project through
spec.dependency_data.project_uuid.

nodes.description: List of nodes in a dependency path.

v1DependencyMetadataPath.description: List of nodes in a dependency path.

v1DependencyScope.description:
- DEPENDENCY_SCOPE_UNSPECIFIED: Dependency scope is undefined.
 - DEPENDENCY_SCOPE_TEST: Dependency is only used for testing.
 - DEPENDENCY_SCOPE_BUILD: Dependency is only used for building the package.
 - DEPENDENCY_SCOPE_NORMAL: Dependency is used in normal, non-test, code.

dependency_name.description: The name of the dependency package.

unpinned.description: Set to true if the dependency is not pinned.

utilization.description: The fraction of the dependency that is used by this package.

v1DependencySpec.description: Captures the details of a single dependency.

dependency_specs.description:
A list of all the related dependencies
for this package or package version.

direct_dependencies.description: The total number of direct dependencies.

indirect_dependencies.description: The total number of indirect dependencies.

count.description: Total count of discussion posts/pull requests.

num_closed.description: Count of closed discussion posts/pull requests.

v1DistributionFormatType.description:
Distribution format type.

 - DISTRIBUTION_FORMAT_TYPE_PYTHON_SOURCE: Python source was used to build this package.
 - DISTRIBUTION_FORMAT_TYPE_PYTHON_EGG: Egg-info based distribution format for python.
 - DISTRIBUTION_FORMAT_TYPE_PYTHON_WHEEL: Dist-info based distribution format for python.

v1Ecosystem.description:
- ECOSYSTEM_GO: GoLang.
 - ECOSYSTEM_MAVEN: Maven.
 - ECOSYSTEM_PYPI: Python.
 - ECOSYSTEM_CARGO: Rust.
 - ECOSYSTEM_NPM: Javascript.
 - ECOSYSTEM_GEM: Ruby.
 - ECOSYSTEM_NUGET: Dotnet.
 - ECOSYSTEM_PACKAGIST: PHP.
 - ECOSYSTEM_SBOM: SBOMs.
 - ECOSYSTEM_RPM: RPM.
 - ECOSYSTEM_DEBIAN: Debian.
 - ECOSYSTEM_GITHUB_ACTION: GitHub Actions.
 - ECOSYSTEM_COCOAPOD: Cocoapods.
 - ECOSYSTEM_APK: APK (alpine et.al).
 - ECOSYSTEM_CONTAINER: Containers.
 - ECOSYSTEM_HUGGING_FACE: Hugging Face.
 - ECOSYSTEM_C: C/C++.
 - ECOSYSTEM_GIT: ecosystem GIT for GIT repository dependencies.
This can be used for package name of the resolved dependencies when a
given repository has dependencies to other GIT repositories. Currently we
use this to represent vulnerabilities for the given GIT repository. ex:
git submodules, C/C++ dependencies.
 - ECOSYSTEM_AI_MODEL: AI models.
 - ECOSYSTEM_SWIFT: Ecosystem Swift consists of native Swift packages, which are defined
using the Package.swift manifest file and managed by the Swift Package
Manager. There is a separate ecosystem for Cocoapod packages called
ECOSYSTEM_COCOAPOD, which is an alternative package manager for Swift
packages.

open_action_template.description: The template to be used for OPEN action for email plugin.

update_action_template.description: The template to be used for UPDATE action for email plugin.

resolve_action_template.description: The template to be used for RESOLVE action for email plugin.

v1EmbeddingModel.description: EmbeddingModel is the model of the embedding provider.

v1EmbeddingProvider.description: EmbeddingProvider is the provider of the embeddings.

v1EmbeddingVector.description: Embedding vector for one code segment.

license_info.description:
An array that stores the types of valid feature licenses and their
limits. Protobuf does not allow enums to be the key of maps. Ensure that
any code accessing this array checks for an existing type before writing.

target_namespace.description: Target namespace of the Endor Labs license.

read_access_until.description:
Optional. Indicates the time upto to which users of this tenant have read
access.

write_access_until.description: The time upto to which users of this tenant have write access.

bundle_info.description: A repeated field containing information about Endor Labs license bundles.

is_customer.description: Indicates whether the license belongs to a customer.

v1EndorTarget.description: EndorTarget determines the origin of the files that will be analyzed by a rule.

v1EndorctlRC.description:
Endorctl return codes.

 - ENDORCTL_RC_SUCCESS: Success (translates to exit code 0).
 - ENDORCTL_RC_ERROR: Unspecified error.
 - ENDORCTL_RC_INVALID_ARGS: Invalid input.
 - ENDORCTL_RC_ENDOR_AUTH_FAILURE: Endor API authentication failure.
 - ENDORCTL_RC_DOCTOR_FAILURE: Endor host-check failure.
 - ENDORCTL_RC_GITHUB_AUTH_FAILURE: GitHub authentication failure.
 - ENDORCTL_RC_ANALYTICS_ERROR: Error running analytics.
 - ENDORCTL_RC_FINDINGS_ERROR: Error generating findings.
 - ENDORCTL_RC_NOTIFICATIONS_ERROR: Error generating notifications.
 - ENDORCTL_RC_GITHUB_API_ERROR: Error calling GitHub API.
 - ENDORCTL_RC_GITHUB_PERMISSIONS_ERROR: Insufficient GitHub token permissions.
 - ENDORCTL_RC_GIT_ERROR: Error while performing a Git operation.
 - ENDORCTL_RC_DEPENDENCY_RESOLUTION_ERROR: Error during dependency resolution.
 - ENDORCTL_RC_DEPENDENCY_SCANNING_ERROR: Error during dependency scanning.
 - ENDORCTL_RC_CALL_GRAPH_ERROR: Error while generating call graphs.
 - ENDORCTL_RC_LINTER_ERROR: Error while running the linter scanner.
 - ENDORCTL_RC_BAD_POLICY_TYPE: A user defined policy has a policy type reserved for system policies.
 - ENDORCTL_RC_POLICY_ERROR: Error evaluating one or more policies.
 - ENDORCTL_RC_INTERNAL_ERROR: Internal error while running endorctl.
 - ENDORCTL_RC_DEADLINE_EXCEEDED: Deadline exceeded while running endorctl.
 - ENDORCTL_RC_NOT_FOUND: Requested entity was not found.
 - ENDORCTL_RC_ALREADY_EXISTS: Attempt to create an entity failed because it already exists.
 - ENDORCTL_RC_UNAUTHENTICATED: Unable to authenticate request.
 - ENDORCTL_RC_VULN_ERROR: Error related to vulnerability ingestion and processing.
 - ENDORCTL_RC_INITIALIZATION_ERROR: Error related to initializing the repository.
 - ENDORCTL_RC_HOST_CHECK_FAILURE: Endor host-check failure.
 - ENDORCTL_RC_SBOM_IMPORT_ERROR: Error importing an sbom.
 - ENDORCTL_RC_PRE_COMMIT_CHECK_FAILURE: A pre-commit check failed.
 - ENDORCTL_RC_GH_ACTION_WORKFLOW_SCAN_FAILURE: GitHub action workflow scan failed.
 - ENDORCTL_RC_FILE_ANALYTICS_ERROR: Error while reading files for analytics processing.
 - ENDORCTL_RC_SIGNATURE_VERIFICATION_FAILURE: Signature verification failure.
 - ENDORCTL_RC_LICENSE_ERROR: The requested operation requires additional licensing.
 - ENDORCTL_RC_HUGGING_FACE_ERROR: Error while running the huggingface scanner.
 - ENDORCTL_RC_SAST_ERROR: Error while running the SAST scanner.
 - ENDORCTL_RC_ARTIFACT_OPERATION_FAILURE: Artifact operation failure.
 - ENDORCTL_RC_SEGMENTATION_ERROR: Error during file segmentation.
 - ENDORCTL_RC_TOOLCHAIN_ERROR: Error generating toolchains.
 - ENDORCTL_RC_SANDBOX_ERROR: Error running endorctl sandbox.
 - ENDORCTL_RC_RULE_SET_ERROR: Error performing rule set operations.
 - ENDORCTL_RC_SECURITY_REVIEW_ERROR: Error while running the PR security review.
 - ENDORCTL_RC_POLICY_VIOLATION: One or more, enforced, admission policy violations detected.
 - ENDORCTL_RC_POLICY_WARNING: One ore more, unenforced, admission policy violations detected.
 - ENDORCTL_RC_PR_SECURITY_REVIEW_ERROR: Deprecated: Use ENDORCTL_RC_SECURITY_REVIEW_ERROR instead.
 - ENDORCTL_RC_EXPORTER_WARNING: Warning while running the exporter. This does not prevent the main scan
from completing.

regex.description: regular expression to match the error message.

fixable.description: Fixable indicates if the error is fixable through configuration.

uuid.description: The UUID of the evaluation method.

propagate.description: Indicates that the object should be visible in child namespaces.

target_kinds.description: The list of resources (kind) to which this evaluation method applies.

uuid.description: The UUID of the evaluation parameter.

propagate.description: Indicates that the object should be visible in child namespaces.

v1EvaluationParameter.description:
EvaluationParameter represents an evaluation parameter.
The parameters are applied to the method before execution.
The parameters correspond to the method identified by the parent UUID.

Order of evaluation:

For system methods, parameters at the system namespace
are used first. If a tenant namespace has parameters for the
same method, they overwrite the system level parameters.

parameters.description: Parameters associated with the evaluation parameter.

policy_uuids.description: List of exception policies triggered by this finding.

tags.description: List of tags set by exception policies.

v1Exceptions.description: Metadata added by the exception policy scanner.

project_uuid.description: The UUID of the project.

repository_version.description:
Repository version is used to select the package versions for the export.
This is an optional field, If not set then the default repository version
will be used.

uuid.description: The UUID of the SBOM.

v1ExportedSBOM.description:
This is the request to the SBOM export API.
It consists of the request resource kind.
The UUID is the UUID of the resource.
The output is the format to export the SBOM.
The SBOM kind is the kind of the SBOM requested by the user. Currently we support only CycloneDx.
The meta parent_kind can take one of two values - PackageVersion or RepositoryVersion.
The Meta parent_uuid corresponds to the parent UUID of the parent kind.

v1ExportedSBOMComponentType.description:
The kind of component for which we are creating an SBOM.
This is a mandatory field.

v1ExportedSBOMFormat.description:
Specifies the output of SBOM. (For example, JSON and XML).

 - FORMAT_JSON: SBOM of JSON format output.
 - FORMAT_XML: SBOM of XML format output.
 - FORMAT_TAG_VALUE: SBOM of Tag Value format output.

data.description:
The output of the SBOM, which is a string type.
For whichever format we generate the SBOM, the output will be a string only response.

hide_private_components.description: Whether to hide internal packages or not.

v1ExportedSBOMSpec.description: The internal specification of the object.

uuid.description: The UUID of the VEX.

v1ExportedVEX.description:
The request to the VEX export API.
It consists of the request resource kind.
The UUID is the uuid of the resource.
The output is the format to export the VEX.
The VEX kind is the kind of the VEX requested by the user. Currently, we support only CycloneDx.

v1ExportedVEXComponentType.description:
The kind of component for which we are creating an SBOM.
This is a mandatory field.

v1ExportedVEXFormat.description:
The output of VEX. (For example, JSON and XML).

 - FORMAT_JSON: SBOM of the JSON format output.
 - FORMAT_XML: SBOM of the XML format output.

data.description: This is the output of the VEX which is a string type only.

v1ExportedVEXSpec.description: The internal specification of the object.

uuid.description: The UUID of the exporter.

propagate.description:
Indicates whether the object should be visible in the child namespaces or
not.

uuid.description: The UUID for the ingested external provider model.

v1ExternalProviderModel.description: ExternalProviderModel represents a model from an external provider.

name.description: The name of the model.

project_uuid.description: The UUID of the project that this model relates to.

terms_of_service.description: The terms of service of the external provider.

v1FactorCategory.description:
Prioritization factors are organized into categories
based on what they capture.

 - FACTOR_CATEGORY_SECURITY_RISK: Security risk related.
 - FACTOR_CATEGORY_OPERATIONAL_RISK: Operational risk related.
 - FACTOR_CATEGORY_ENTERPRISE_CONTEXT: Captures the enterprize context, such as critical projects.
 - FACTOR_CATEGORY_COST_OF_FIXING: Captures the effort required to fix the issue.
 - FACTOR_CATEGORY_RISK_OF_FIXING: Captures the risk of attempting to fix the issue.
 - FACTOR_CATEGORY_IMPACT_OF_FIXING: Captures the level of improvement gained from fixing the issue.
 - FACTOR_CATEGORY_FEASIBILITY_OF_FIXING: Captures how feasible it is to fix the issue.

v1FactorImpact.description:
The settings capture the impact of this factor on the decision.

 - FACTOR_IMPACT_NONE: Does not affect the priority.
 - FACTOR_IMPACT_POSITIVE: Important. Increases the priority.
 - FACTOR_IMPACT_VERY_POSITIVE: Very important. Increases the priority even more.
 - FACTOR_IMPACT_NEGATIVE: Not important. Reduces the priority.
 - FACTOR_IMPACT_VERY_NEGATIVE: Not important at all. Reduces the priority even more.

resource_uuid.description: The unique identifier (UUID) of the parent resource to which this feedback is attached.

resource_kind.description: The type of the parent resource to which this feedback is attached, such as "Project".

comment.description: The comment associated with the feedback.

uuid.description: The UUID of the filehash query request.

v1FileHashQueryRequest.description:
A file hash query request allows the caller to send a set of file hashes
and use the segment index to match those hashes to file versions
in one or more repositories.

v1FileInfo.description: The breakdown for various file categories.

uuid.description: UUID of the file segments.

v1FileSegments.description:
File segment metadata for a given customer source file.
Stored in mongo and used to refresh dependencies.
NOTE: Not used for OSS-projects.

project_uuid.description: UUID of the project that this file belongs to.

has_codeowners.description: Whether the version contain a valid code owners file or not.

has_license.description: Whether there is a licence or not.

has_security_md.description: Whether the version contain a SECURITY.md file or not.

documentation_files.description: The names of files that contain documentation.

test_files.description: The names of files that contain tests.

cicd_files.description: The names of files that contain CI/CD configuration.

best_practices_files.description: The names of files that represent best practices.

sast_files.description: The names of files that are related to SAST tools.

make_files.description: The names of files that are related to Make tools.

badges.description: The names of the badges in the repository.

file_types.description: The breadown for each file type.

languages.description: The breadown for each language type.

copyrights.description: The list of copyright notices found in source files.

v1FileStats.description: File statistics message. Included as the payload of a metric object.

uuid.description: The UUID of the object.

v1Finding.description:
A finding contains details of a problem that needs to be fixed.
The finding applies to the parent object, which can be one of:
Repository, RepositoryVersion, or PackageVersion.
Finding objects are connected to the project via spec.project_uuid.

v1FindingCategory.description:
Finding categories loosely correspond to use cases.

 - FINDING_CATEGORY_VULNERABILITY: Vulnerability.
 - FINDING_CATEGORY_SUPPLY_CHAIN: Supply chain specific problem (malicious packages, typosquats).
 - FINDING_CATEGORY_LICENSE_RISK: License issue.
 - FINDING_CATEGORY_SCPM: Security posture management.
 - FINDING_CATEGORY_SECURITY: Generic security issue.
 - FINDING_CATEGORY_OPERATIONAL: Generic operational issue.
 - FINDING_CATEGORY_SECRETS: Exposed secret.
 - FINDING_CATEGORY_MALWARE: Malware.
 - FINDING_CATEGORY_CICD: CI/CD pipeline issue.
 - FINDING_CATEGORY_TOOLS: Tooling issue.
 - FINDING_CATEGORY_GHACTIONS: Finding applies to a GitHub action dependency.
 - FINDING_CATEGORY_CONTAINER: Finding applies to a container image.
 - FINDING_CATEGORY_SAST: SAST.
 - FINDING_CATEGORY_AI_MODELS: AI Models.
 - FINDING_CATEGORY_SECURITY_REVIEW: Security review.
 - FINDING_CATEGORY_SCA: Software Composition Analysis issue.

layer_digests.description:
List of layer digests for the container image where the target
dependency was found.

has_base_layer.description:
Set to true if the target dependency was found in the base layer of the
container image.

base_image.description:
Base image of the container image where the target dependency was
found.

v1FindingFixingPatch.description: FindingFixingPatch that can fix the finding.

upgrade_list.description: List of upgrades that can fix the finding.

v1FindingFixingUpgrades.description: Upgrades that can fix the finding.

uuid.description: The UUID of the finding log.

v1FindingLog.description:
A finding log contains details of the state of a finding at the time it was
created, updated or deleted.

finding_parent_kind.description: Finding parent object resource kind. For example, PackageVersion.

days_unresolved.description: Number of days that this finding remained unresolved.

target_uuid.description: The UUID of the DependencyMetadata object for the dependency.

target_dependency_package_name.description:
Fully qualified name of the dependency. For example,
eco://package@version.

finding_tags.description:
List of tags, or attributes, that describe the scope of the finding
and can be used to filter findings.

finding_categories.description: List of categories that capture the use case to which the finding fits.

approximation.description:
True if this finding is for an approximate dependency
based on the unresolved package dependencies.

finding_parent_name.description: finding_parent_name is the name of the parent object.

name.description: Policy name.

uuid.description: Policy UUID.

v1FindingPolicyInfo.description: Details about the policy that created the finding.

v1FindingRemediation.description:
Recommended action to resolve the finding.

 - FINDING_REMEDIATION_UPGRADE: Upgrade to a later version.
 - FINDING_REMEDIATION_DOWNGRADE: Downgrade to an older version.
 - FINDING_REMEDIATION_REPLACE: Replace dependency with another package.
 - FINDING_REMEDIATION_REMOVE: Remove unused dependency.
 - FINDING_REMEDIATION_VENDOR: Vendor a dependency.
 - FINDING_REMEDIATION_IMPROVE: Make changes to improve a dependency.
 - FINDING_REMEDIATION_REIMPLEMENT: Reimplement a dependency locally.
 - FINDING_REMEDIATION_REVIEW: Review, no remediation to suggest
 - FINDING_REMEDIATION_NOTIFICATION: Notification, there may not be any remediation
 - FINDING_REMEDIATION_PIN: Pin dependency to one of the recommended versions

project_uuid.description: The UUID of the project to which this finding belongs.

last_processed.description: Last time the finding was processed.

dismiss.description:
Set to true to exclude finding from action policies (a.k.a. admission and
notification policies).

remediation.description: String describing the recommended remediation to fix this finding.

summary.description: A more detailed description of the finding.

finding_tags.description:
List of tags, or attributes, that describe the scope of the finding
and can be used to filter findings.

target_uuid.description:
The UUID of the DependencyMetadata object for the dependency, if
applicable.

extra_key.description:
Additional information used to create a unique finding.

In some cases we want to create multiple findings for the same
combination of parent_uuid, meta.name, and target_uuid, for example when
there are multiple vulnerabilities affecting the same dependency. By
setting different values in this field, we are able to create a unique
finding per combination of parent_uuid, meta.name, target_uuid, and
extra_key.

target_dependency_package_name.description:
Fully qualified name of the dependency, e.g. eco://package@version, if
applicable.

target_dependency_name.description:
Dependency package name, if applicable.
This is just the name (i.e. it does not include the ecosystem or the
version).

target_dependency_version.description:
Dependency version, if applicable.
This is just the version (i.e. it does not include the ecosystem or the
package name).

explanation.description: Information about why this finding is considered noteworthy.

reachable_paths.description:
Function paths to the vulnerable method.
Only applies to vulnerability findings.

finding_categories.description: List of categories that capture the use case the finding fits in.

relationship.description:
String describing the relationship to dependency,
For example, "Foo is a direct dependency of bar.", if applicable.

latest_version.description: Latest version of dependency, if available.

dependency_file_paths.description:
List of relative paths to the dependency files used to create the bom, if
applicable. This field is optional and is only set when the source code
of the package version is known. For example, for Golang it contains
go.mod and go.sum.

approximation.description:
True if this finding is for an approximate dependency
based on the unresolved package dependencies.

proposed_version.description: Recommended version of dependency if available.

location_urls.description: The URLs that correspond to the paths contained in dependency_file_paths.

v1FindingSummaryInfo.description: FindingSummaryInfo contains some selected fields for a finding.

v1FindingTags.description:
Finding attributes.

 - FINDING_TAGS_DIRECT: Finding applies to a direct dependency.
 - FINDING_TAGS_TRANSITIVE: Finding applies to a transitive (indirect) dependency.
 - FINDING_TAGS_PROJECT_INTERNAL: Finding applies to a dependency that belongs to the same project.
 - FINDING_TAGS_NAMESPACE_INTERNAL: Finding applies to a dependency that belongs to the same namespace.
 - FINDING_TAGS_REACHABLE_DEPENDENCY: Finding applies to a reachable dependency.
 - FINDING_TAGS_UNREACHABLE_DEPENDENCY: Finding applies to an unreachable dependency.
 - FINDING_TAGS_POTENTIALLY_REACHABLE_DEPENDENCY: Finding applies to a potentially reachable dependency.
 - FINDING_TAGS_REACHABLE_FUNCTION: Finding applies to a reachable function.
 - FINDING_TAGS_UNREACHABLE_FUNCTION: Finding applies to an unreachable function.
 - FINDING_TAGS_POTENTIALLY_REACHABLE_FUNCTION: Finding applies to a potentially reachable function.
 - FINDING_TAGS_UNFIXABLE: Finding is unfixable.
 - FINDING_TAGS_TEST: Finding applies to a dependency not in production code.
 - FINDING_TAGS_NORMAL: Finding applies to a normal, non-test, dependency.
 - FINDING_TAGS_FIX_AVAILABLE: There is a fix available for the CVE reported in this finding.
 - FINDING_TAGS_SELF: Finding applies only to the analyzed package version, there is no
dependency involved.
 - FINDING_TAGS_POLICY: Finding was generated by a policy.
 - FINDING_TAGS_CI_BLOCKER: Finding caused a CI failure.
 - FINDING_TAGS_VALID_SECRET: Finding applies to a valid secret.
 - FINDING_TAGS_INVALID_SECRET: Finding applies to an invalid secret.
 - FINDING_TAGS_PATH_EXTERNAL: Finding applies to a transitive dependency that can only be reached via
external, non-OSS, project paths.
 - FINDING_TAGS_MALWARE: Finding applies to malicious package.
 - FINDING_TAGS_UNDER_REVIEW: Finding applies to suspicious package under review.
 - FINDING_TAGS_PHANTOM: Finding applies to a phantom dependency.
 - FINDING_TAGS_EXCEPTION: Finding is exempt from action policies.
 - FINDING_TAGS_CI_WARNING: Finding caused a CI warning.
 - FINDING_TAGS_NOTIFICATION: Finding triggered a notification.
 - FINDING_TAGS_EXPLOITED: This vulnerability is known to be exploited.
 - FINDING_TAGS_DISPUTED: This vulnerability has been marked as 'disputed'.
 - FINDING_TAGS_WITHDRAWN: This vulnerability has been marked as 'withdrawn'.

reduction.description: The reduction in findings across all severities.

current_count.description: The finding count in the current version across all severities.

upgrade_count.description: The finding count in the upgrade version across all severities.

severity.description: The breakdown of finding information per severity level.

current_count.description: The finding count in the current version.

upgrade_count.description: The finding count in the upgrade version.

fixed_summary.description: The findings fixed. Summary information only.

introduced_summary.description: The findings introduced. Summary information only.

fixed_count.description: The number of total findings fixed by the upgrade.

introduced_count.description: The number of total findings introduced by the upgrade.

reachable_fixed_count.description: The number of reachable findings fixed by the upgrade.

reachable_introduced_count.description: The number of reachable findings introduced by the upgrade.

uuid.description: The UUID of the function graph.

zstd_bytes.description:
Function graph proto bytes are encoded in zstd bytes.
It is the responsibility of the caller to compress/uncompress the bytes.

related_object.description: The internal value that identifies the function graph storage location.

storage_url.description: The URL to the storage location of the callgraph.

v1FunctionGraphData.description: FunctionGraphData represents a function graph data request in the system.

v1Fuzzy.description:
Fuzzy is used to search for a similar string. Parameters
determine the deviation.

priority.description:
The priority of the Ruby gems package manager.
Priorities are relative. If multiple package managers
have the same priority, the order between them can be random.
Lower number means higher priority.

url.description: The URL of the registry that the Ruby gems package manager must use.

user.description: The user name to use to access this repository.

password.description: An encrypted password that must be provided by the repository owner.

v1GemSpec.description: GemSpec represents a configuration for the Ruby gems package manager.

mask.description: List of fields to return (all fields are returned by default).

app_id.description: The app id of the installation.

enable_pr_scans.description:
enable_pr_scans is a boolean value that indicates if the automated PR scans
are enabled.

enable_pr_comments.description:
enable_pr_comments is a boolean value that indicates if the automated PR
comments should be enabled.

enable_full_scan.description:
enable_full_scan is a boolean value that indicates if the full scan should
be enabled.

include_archived_repos.description:
include_archived_repos is a boolean value that indicates if the archived
repos should be included. Default will be off.

installation_github_user.title: installation_github_user is the Github user that initiated the installation

host_url.description: The base URL of the corresponding SCM app.

pull_request_payload.description:
pull_request_payload contains the GitHub Pull Request payload
associated with this scan request, when available. This allows downstream
components (e.g., security review scanner) to avoid refetching PR data
from GitHub and instead map this JSON to internal structures on demand.

v1GitHubWorkflows.description: GitHubWorkflows Metric Value contains information about the workflow file's code posture management.

personal_access_token.description: PAT of the user doing integration to fetch resources using REST APIs.

webhook_secret.description: The webhook secret configured in GitLab for validating incoming webhook events.

enable_mr_scans.description:
enable_mr_scans is a boolean value that indicates if the automated MR scans
are enabled.

enable_mr_comments.description:
enable_mr_comments is a boolean value that indicates if the automated MR
comments should be enabled.

http_clone_url.description:
The HTTP clone URL.

The following examples show valid URLs.

https://github.com/yarpc/yarpc-go.git

http://github.com/yarpc/yarpc-go.git

https://my.github.com/a/b.git

https://www.gitlab.com/a/b/c.git

https://github.com/eclipse-vertx/vert.x.git

https://github.com/jboss/jboss-ejb-api_spec.git

The URL is stored as lower case letters to normalize searches.

git_clone_url.description:
The Git clone URL.
For example, git://github.com/yarpc/yarpc-go.git.

The URL is stored as lower case letters to normalize searches.

organization.description:
The organization where the repository is located.

For example, yarpc

GitHub: curl https://api.github.com/repos/yarpc/yarpc-go | jq
.organization.login

path.description:
The source control repository in the project without the organization.

For example, yarpc-go

GitHub: curl https://api.github.com/repos/yarpc/yarpc-go | jq .name

full_name.description:
Represents the Git namespace and its associated hierarchy.

The format is "namespace/name", comprising the organization name and
repository name.

For example, yarpc/yarpc-go

GitHub: curl https://api.github.com/repos/yarpc/yarpc-go | jq .full_name

web_url.description:
The web URL to view the repository.

For example, https://github.com/yarpc/yarpc-go

GitHub: curl https://api.github.com/repos/yarpc/yarpc-go | jq .html_url

The URL is stored as lower case letters to normalize searches.

external_installation_id.description:
Endor Labs GitHub app installation ID of this project.
Optional and only available if the project is created through an
installation.

invalid_installation.description:
Indicates that Endor Labs installation no longer exists
for this project and was potentially deleted. Endor Labs can no longer
refresh or rescan this project.

v1GitSpec.description:
Git repository project configuration.

Fields of this object might differ from the Git host.

client_id.description: The client ID of the GitHub app.

client_secret.description: The client secret of the GitHub app.

webhook_secret.description: The webhook secret of the GitHub app.

pem.description: The PEM file of the GitHub app. This is the private key of the GitHub app.

html_url.description: The HTML URL of the GitHub app.

app_name.description: The name of the GitHub app.

token.description: The address fields of the tenant.

property_key_name.description: The key name for Gradle properties.

property_key_value.description: The key value for Gradle properties.

v1GradleSpec.description: GradleSpec represents properties that need to be set for Gradle projects.

v1GroupAggregationValueResponse.description:
GroupAggregationValueResponse returns the value of the aggregation if
requested.

v1GroupResponse.description: Response to a list group request.

sha256.description: Checksum value of package in hash 256 algorithm.

sha512.description: Checksum value of package in hash 512 algorithm.

v1Hash.description: Sequence of characters that can help you verify data integrity.

secret.description: The secret to use for the HMAC calculation.

hdr_name.description: The name of the header field that contains the signature.

authz.title:
If set, render the key-value as "Authorization: Key Value"
instead of just "Key: Value". The supported keys are the ones
specified in the Authorization enum (as string)

separator.description: The separator to use between key and value. Default is ":".

v1HttpParam.description:
HttpParam is the key/value pair that will be rendered as "Key: Value"
in the section of the requested portion of the HTTP request. It could
be part of the request like the header, the query, or the body.

If the authz flag is set, the library will render it as
"Authorization: Key Value" instead of just "Key: Value".

name.title: Name identifies this entry. For example, "GitHub"

description.description: Detailed description of the request. For example, "GitHub credential validation".

request_body.description:
Request body as a string. This will be sent as the body of the HTTP request.
If both request_body and the body parameter in SendRequest are provided,
the body parameter takes precedence.

v1HttpParamsProfile.description: HttpParamsProfile defines the parameters to create an HTTP request.

uri.title: The URI to use for this validation. For example, "https://api.github.com/user"

method.description: Method is either GET or POST.

header.description: The information to be put in the HTTP header.

query.description: The information to be put in the HTTP query section.

body.description: The information to be put in the HTTP body.

method_name.description:
Gets appended to the URL as:
URL[:method_name] usually followed by the query section.

v1HttpParamsRequest.description: HttpParamsRequest contains the information expected with the HTTP request.

status_code.description: The code expected on success. For example, 200.

invalid_code_regex.description: The pattern/regex expected on failure. For example, "5\\d\\d".

successful_auth_codes.description: The codes expected for a successful authentication.

failed_auth_codes.description: The codes expected on the failed authentication.

successful_response_data.description:
Map of key-value pairs that must be present in the response body for successful validation.
This allows validation beyond just HTTP status codes.
Example: {"ok": "true", "status": "active"} would require the response to contain both keys
with matching values. The response can contain additional fields.

v1HttpParamsResponse.description: HttpParamsResponse contains the information expected with the HTTP response.

uuid.description: The UUID for the ingested HuggingFace model.

v1HuggingFaceModel.description: HuggingFaceModel represents the JSON object from ingesting from the HuggingFace API.

name.description: Author and model name (id). For example, "openai/clip-vit-base-patch16".

model_id.description: Author and model name (seems to hold the same value as the name field).

author.description: Author of the model. For example, "openai".

sha.description: Repository SHA at this particular revision.

last_modified.description: Date of last commit to the repository.

private.description: Whether the repository is private not.

disabled.description: Whether the repository is disabled or not.

pipeline_tag.description: Pipeline tag associated with the model.

tags.description: List of tags of the model.

downloads.description: Number of downloads of the model.

mask_token.description: Mask token used by the model.

widget_data.description: Widget data associated with the model.

likes.description: Number of likes of the model.

transformers_info.description: Transformers-specific information associated with the model.

spaces.description: List of spaces using the model.

siblings.description: List of files that constitute the model.

created_at.description: Date of creation of the repository on the Hub.

authorization_required.description: Requires authorized request to access the model.

project_uuid.description: The UUID of the project that this model relates to.

uuid.description: The UUID of the ip address policy.

propagate.description:
Propagate indicates that the object should be visible in child namespaces.
It should be set to true for most use cases.

v1IPAddressPolicy.description:
An IPAddressPolicy creates a restriction on which IP address subnets can
access a tenant.
Note that only one IP address policy is allowed per tenant and IP address
policies cannot be declared in child namespaces.

allowed_ip_subnets.description:
The list of allowed IP subnets from where traffic should be
allowed.

uuid.description: The UUID of an identify provider.

propagate.description: Propagate indicates that the object should be visible in child namespaces.

uuid.description: The UUID of the object.

v1ImportedRuleSet.description: ImportedRuleSet represents a request to import a set of rules.

sha256_hash.description: The SHA256 checksum if applicable (e.g, file).

bytes.description: A file has been used as a source.

version.description: The version for this rule set. All rules in the set will be created with this version.

v1ImportedRuleSetSpec.description: The internal specification of the object.

v1ImportedSBOM.description: ImportedSBOM represents an SBOM that has been imported.

identifier.description:

For example, a CycloneDX SBOM will have metadata.component.bom-ref as the identifier of the main component.

main_component_purl.description: The PURL of the main component of a SBOM.

supplier_name.description: The suplier name of the imported SBOM.

cyclone_dx.description: CycloneDX is the cyclonedx bom struct serialized to a string.

spdx.description: SPDX is the spdx bom struct serialized to a string.

cyclone_dx_bytes.description: CycloneDX bom struct encoded in zstd bytes.

spdx_bytes.description: SPDX bom struct encoded in zstd bytes.

v1ImportedSBOMSpec.description: The internal specification of the object.

ingestion_time.description: Time the object was last ingested.
