Raw ingested object from a 3rd party source,
along with any additional metadata we want
to add to the object.

uuid.description: The UUID of the installation.

propagate.description: Propagate indicates that the object should be visible in child namespaces.

v1Installation.description: Installation represents an installation request in the system.

public.description: Apply only to public repositories. Default value is false.

external_id.description: The external ID of the installation.

external_name.description: The external name of the installation.

user.description:
The user name of the user that initiated the installation.
Refers back to the GitHub user name.

ingestion_time.description: The last time that we ingested the installation data.

target_type.description: The target of the installation (Organization or User).

suspended.description: Indicates if the installation is suspended.

project_uuids.description: The list of projects that are associated with this installation.

login.description:
The login of the account taken directly from the GitHub response
VS spec.user, which is inferred from the identity token in the request
context.

invalid.description:
Identifies the installations that are in the system,
but potentially their corresponding configuration has been removed
from GitHub and thus we cannot authenticate. In this case,
we stop trying to re-sync with this installation.

ingestion_token.description:
The API token that the scanner can use for scanning installation
related information. If it is empty, the scanner will use local
authentication information.

enabled_features.title: Enabled features. The valid values are "git, github"

marked_for_deletion.description:
Indicates the installation is marked for deletion,
and in next sync call the installation with its related data will be
deleted.

include_archived_repos.description:
include_archived_repos is a boolean value that indicates if the archived
repos should be included. Default will be off.

installation_error_message.description:
Message explaining why the installation is invalid.
This field is populated when invalid is true.

uuid.description: The UUID of the invitation.

v1Invitation.description: Invitation represents an invitation for a new user in the system.

user_email.description: The email of the user that we want to invite to the tenant.

user_name.description: The user name to authenticate with Jira.

api_key.description: The API Key to authenticate with Jira.

project_key.description: The project key in which the Jira issue is raised as an action for the notification.

labels.description: The labels to apply to the Jira issue.

resolve_state.description: The final ticket status after resolving.

use_bearer_token.description: Denotes if we should use bearer token to authenticate with Jira.

bearer_token.description: The bearer token to authenticate with Jira.

components.description: The Components field in Jira board.

uuid.description: The UUID of the jvm class metadata.

v1JVMClassMetadata.description: JVMClassMetadata contains metadata such as file sha, name, gav of oss library for a jvm class file.

file_name.description:
Name of java class file.
This would be the fully qualified class name, such as org.slf4j.ILoggerFactory.

gav.description:
Open source library that contains this class file, in endor ecosystem name format.
eg for org.slf4j.ILoggerFactory, this value would be mvn://org.slf4j:slf4j-api@1.7.

is_original_sha.description:
True indicates if the class file sha value is the original class's sha value.
False indicates the sha value would be for a class that is remapped, i.e namespace wiped off.

is_rebundled.description: True indicates if the class file is a dependency class file which has been rebundled within this package.

classes.description: List of classes in the Java package.

import_statements.description: List of external classes referenced in the Java package.

v1JVMSourceMeta.description: Contains metadata information about source code for a Java package.

source_uuid.description: UUID passed back to the client for which the job is being performed.

start_time.description: Start time of the job denotes the time when the job is scheduled for execution.

end_time.description: End time of the job denotes the time when the job execution is completed.

last_heartbeat_time.description: Last Heartbeat time of the job.

max_duration.description: Maximum duration of the job. Defaults to 5 minutes. Cannot exceed 60 minutes.

max_retries.description: Maximum number of retries for the job. Defaults to 1. Maximum allowed is 5.

retention_time.description:
Retention time of the job. Defaults to 30 days.
Cannot exceed 1095 days.

run_errors_history.description:
run errors history of the job. This is maintained
for debugging purposes.

unstructured_content.description: The unstructured content of the LLM context.

v1Languages.description: The languages used and bytes of code for each language in the source control repository.

all_licenses.description: All the licenses found by Endor Labs.

code_licenses.description: The licenses found in the source code.

package_manager_licenses.description: The licenses from the package manager.

copyrights.description: The list of copyright notices found in source files.

v1LicenseData.description:
Contains a summary of license information for a project or package.
This will be included as the payload of a metric object.

name.description:
Raw license description as found, for example in package managers.
It is free form text that may not contain a valid SPDX ID.

type.description: License classification (based on licenseclassifier by Google).

url.description: The URL that points to the license description.

file.description: The name of the file where the license was found.

file_location.description: The line in the file where the license text begins.

matched_text.description: The license text that was matched.

v1LicenseInfo.description: LicenseInfo contains information for a license.

v1LicenseMappingInfo.description:
LicenseMappingInfo contains additional information
that we determine when we attempt to match a license string
to a known SPDX id.

 - LICENSE_MAPPING_INFO_NOT_OSS: This does not look to like an OSS license.
 - LICENSE_MAPPING_INFO_IN_CODE: License information is to be found in the code.
 - LICENSE_MAPPING_INFO_UNKNOWN: Cannot determine anything about the license.
 - LICENSE_MAPPING_INFO_NOT_INCOMPLETE: There is some license information, but is not detailed enough.
 - LICENSE_MAPPING_INFO_PRIVATE: Appears to be a private package.
 - LICENSE_MAPPING_INFO_EXACT: An exact match to a SPDX id.
 - LICENSE_MAPPING_INFO_UNLICENSED: Seems to be explicitly without license.

v1LicenseSource.description:
Enum to describe the source of  license information.

 - LICENSE_SOURCE_UNSPECIFIED: Licesne Source unspecified
 - LICENSE_SOURCE_PKG_MGR_ONLY: License was found only in the package manager metadata,
but not in the source code. This may happen if source code is unavailable
or scanning failed. In this case, spec.license_data contains only package manager license(s).
 - LICENSE_SOURCE_PKG_MGR_AND_CODE: License was found in both the code and the package manager.
spec.license_data contains all licenses, and the Metric includes the package manager license.
 - LICENSE_SOURCE_CODE_ONLY: License was found only in the code , not in the package manager.
spec.license_data will not include any package manager license.
 - LICENSE_SOURCE_NOT_FOUND: No license data found anywhere

v1LicenseSpec.description: Specification mostly follows the SPDX data format.

version.description: The version of the linter correctness analysis.

description.description: The textual description of the linter correctness analysis.

sanitizers.description: The sanitizers used to determine the correctness of the linter result.

code_fixes.description: Code fixes are potential fixes to the identified issue.

v1LinterCorrectnessAnalysis.description: LinterCorrectnessAnalysis represents an analysis of a linter result.

uuid.description: The UUID of a linter result.

v1LinterResult.description: The meta parent_kind can take one of two values - PackageVersion or RepositoryVersion.

rule_id.description: The rule that generated the result.

file.description: The file where the result was found.

line.description: The line in the file where the result was found.

critical_api_category.description: The critical API category of the result.

snippet.description: The source code that triggered the result.

v1LinterResultInfo.description: A brief summary of linter results.

project_uuid.description: The UUID of the project to which this result is related.

extra_key.description:
Additional information that may result in creating a unique linter result.
In some cases we may receive multiple results for the same project and the same file and we
want to have multiple entries for the same field like parent_uuid, meta.name, and physical_location.
By setting different values in this field, we will be able to create a separate result.
Essentially, we will have a unique entry per field.

fingerprints.description:
The list and count of found fingerprints. The counter is maintained
to ease the retrieval of these records in the analytics phase.

ref.description: The Git reference of the repository version.

storage_location.description: The storage location of the package related to this linter result.

suppressed.description: Result was suppressed by semgrep (e.g. because of a "nosemgrep" code annotation).

linter_correctness_analyses.description: An analysis of the linter result.

files_with_result_count.description: The number of files with results.

distinct_rules_with_results.description: The count of distint rules with results.

distinct_file_types_with_results.description: The count of distinct file types with results.

results_per_filecnt_ratio_by_severity.description: The ratio of result count/file count per severity level.

results_per_loc_ratio_by_severity.description: The ratio of result count/LOC per severity level.

results_per_filecnt_ratio_by_file_type.description: The ratio of result count/file count per file type.

results_per_loc_ratio_by_file_type.description: The ratio of result count/LOC per severity per file type.

results_cnt_by_severity.description: The breakdown of result count per severity level.

results_cnt_by_rule.description: The breakdown of result count per rule.

results_cnt_by_file_type.description: The breakdown of result count per file type.

results_cnt_by_category.description: The breakdown of result count per category.

file_cnt_by_severity.description: The breakdown of distinct file count per severity level.

file_cnt_by_rule.description: The breakdown of distinct file count per rule.

file_cnt_by_file_type.description: The breakdown of distinct file count per file type.

file_cnt_by_category.description: The breakdown of distinct file count per category.

results.description: A list of all the linter results.

results_cnt_by_critical_api_category.description: The breakdown of result count per critical API category.

file_cnt_by_critical_api_category.description: The breakdown of distinct file count per critical API category.

finding_results.description:
A list of the results that are marked with ANALYTICS_FINDING, which results
in raising findings.

filter.description:
String of conditions to filter resources by.
Filters may include any attribute along with the following operators:
"==", "!=", ">", ">=", "<", "<=", "Contains", "In", "Matches", and
"Exists". The logical operators "And" and "Or" are also supported.
Expressions may be combined and grouped with parentheses.

Examples:
"spec.value == 5",
"spec.value in ["a", "b", "c"]",
"(meta.name == "xyz" and "spec.value in ["a","b"]) or (spec.value ==
"c")".

page_token.description:
Set the page token to start from.
Use page tokens to page through list results or list specific pages.

page_size.description:
Set the page size to limit the number of results returned.
Default: 100. Max: 500.

action.description: Action to be executed with a request. Not supported for all endpoints.

mask.description: List of fields to return (all fields are returned by default).

traverse.description: Get data from any child namespaces as well.

count.description:
Return the number of objects matching the given list parameters.
If count is set to true, the response is a CountResponse.
Can be used together with filter and traverse.

ci_run_uuid.description: Only return objects from PR scans that match this context id.

disable_pagination.description:
If true, the results will not be paginated and
only the first page will be returned. The order
of the results is not guaranteed.

name.description:
The (preferably) unique name of the code location.
Could be a function ref or a module name.

type.description: The type of the code location.

v1LocationWithName.description: A code location with a name and type.

v1LogLevel.description:
- LOG_LEVEL_EMERGENCY: System is unusable, for example, a panic condition.
 - LOG_LEVEL_ALERT: Action must be taken immediately, for example, a corrupted system
database.
 - LOG_LEVEL_CRITICAL: Critical conditions such as hard device errors.
 - LOG_LEVEL_ERROR: Error conditions.
 - LOG_LEVEL_WARNING: Warning conditions.
 - LOG_LEVEL_NOTICE: Normal but significant conditions that may require special handling.
 - LOG_LEVEL_INFO: Informational messages that confirmation that the program is working as
expected.
 - LOG_LEVEL_DEBUG: Messages that contain information normally of use only for debugging.

parameters.description: parameters that are been passed to the tool.

has_error.description: whether the tool call has an error.

client_cert.description: The client certificate in PEM format.

client_key.description: The client key in PEM format.

server_cert.description: The server certificate in PEM format.

ca_cert.description: The CA certificate in PEM format.

uuid.description: The UUID of a malware record.

pkg_release_date.description: Release date of the package version.

malware_detected_on.description: Date when the malware was detected.

advisory_published.description: Date when the advisory published the malware record.

advisory_last_updated.description: Timestamp of the last update of the malware record by the advisory.

reasons.description: Reasons for flagging the package as malicious.

purl.description: PURL of the package without the version component.

contested.description: Has the malware record been contested.

contested_reason.description: Reason for the malware record being contested.

contested_date.description: Date when the malware record was contested.

references.description: Reference URLs for the malware record.

additional_notes.description: Additional notes for the malware record.

cwe_id.description: The CWE ID for the malware record.

priority.description:
The priority of the Maven package manager.
Priorities are relative. If multiple package managers
have the same priority, the order between them can be random.
Lower number means higher priority.

url.description: The URL of the registry that the Maven package manager must use.

user.description: The user name to use to access this repo.

password.title:
An encrypted password that must be provided by the repository owner.
https://maven.apache.org/guides/mini/guide-encryption.html

id.description:
A string that uniquely identifies this proxy/server. This is the
UUID of the object if not provided.

name.description:
User identifiable name for the repository. This is the name
of the package manager if not provided.

snapshots.description: Indicates whether the repository can contain snapshot versions.

is_plugin_repository.description: Indicates whether the repository is a plugin or binary repository.

region.title: Region to be used for cloud storage integration

public_repository.description: Indicates if cloud storage is accessible publicly.

v1MavenSpec.description: MavenSpec represents a configuration for the Maven package manager.

create_time.description:
Time the resource was created.

Format: 2017-01-15T01:30:15.01Z
RFC 3339: https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc3339.txt.

update_time.description:
Time the resource was last updated.
Note: Updated on all create/patch/delete operations.

Format: 2017-01-15T01:30:15.01Z
RFC 3339: https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc3339.txt.

upsert_time.description:
Time the resource was last upserted.

Note:
create_time is only set the first time the resource is created.
upsert_time is set every time the resource is upseted.

Format: 2017-01-15T01:30:15.01Z
RFC 3339: https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc3339.txt.

name.description: Resource name. Must be 63 characters or less.

kind.description:
Resource kind, for example, HelloResponse.
Auto-generated using the protobuf message proto.MessageName().Name().

description.description: Resource description. Must be less than 1024 bytes.

parent_kind.description: Parent object resource kind, for example, Project.

tags.description:
List of tags attached to the resource.
Tags can be used to select objects and to find collections of objects that
satisfy certain conditions. A tag must be 63 characters or less.

annotations.description:
Annotations can be used to attach metadata to a resource message.
Annotation values can be small or large, structured or unstructured,
and may include characters not permitted by labels.
The keys may contain alphanumerics, underscores (_), dots (.) and dashes
(-). The values of an annotation must be 16384 bytes or smaller.

created_by.description:
Name and authentication source of the user who created the object,
for example, ewok@endor.ai@google@api-key.

updated_by.description:
Name and authentication source of the last user who updated the object,
for example, vulnerabilityingestor@endor.ai@x509.

references.description: Map of objects referenced in a query API.

v1Meta.description: Common fields for all Endor Labs resources.

v1Method.description:
The type of allowed action.

 - METHOD_READ: METHOD_READ is for LIST/GET
 - METHOD_CREATE: METHOD_CREATE is for POST
 - METHOD_UPDATE: METHOD_UPDATE is for PATCH
 - METHOD_DELETE: METHOD_DELETE is for DELETE
 - METHOD_ALL: METHOD_ALL is for all methods (essentially *)

uuid.description: The UUID of the metric.

v1MetricConfiguration.description: This message contains the definition all metrics configurations.

type.description: Type is the kind of metric available.

enabled.description: Whether this metric is enabled in the current configuration or not.

category.description: The categories of metrics available for this kind.

options.description:
The parameteres used for the calculations. This is
a read only object at this time.

v1MetricConfigurationSpec.description: The internal specification of the metric configuration.

analytic.description: The name of the analytic used to generate this specific metric.

project_uuid.description: The UUID of the project to which this metric relates.

metric_values.description:
A map of metric values.
These values include scores and score factors.

raw.description: This is a superset of the information included in the specification.

v1MetricValue.description: The value of the metric and the associated category.

full_name.description:
The author and model name.
For example, author/model_name.

http_clone_url.description:
The HTTP clone URL of the project.
For example, https://huggingface.co/openai-community/gpt2.

priority.description:
The priority of the Node package manager.
Priorities are relative. If multiple package managers
have the same priority, the order between them can be random.
Lower number means higher priority.

url.description: The URL of the registry that NPM must use.

token.description: A base64 encoded user name and password as token.

scope.title:
A private registry scope.
Ex: @myorg/pkg
myorg is the scope

auth_token.description: An auth token that cloud providers typically provide to connect to the registry.

NVD Vulnerability.
Based on schema definition provided by NVD here. https://csrc.nist.gov/schema/nvd/api/2.0/cve_api_json_2.0.schema.

Namespace provides a mechanism for isolating groups of resources.
Namespaces may be defined in a parent child hierarchy.

full_name.description:
The fully qualified name of a namespace,
which identifies the namespace and the associated
hierarchy. For example, endord.dev.domain.
The field is auto-generated at creation.

managed.description: Managed indicates that the object is managed by the system.

uuid.description: The UUID of a notification.

v1Notification.description: The record created when there is a policy violation.

open_action_complete.description: The value is true when action related to opening a new notification action is successfully executed.

update_action_complete.description: The value is true when action related to updating an existing notification is successfully executed.

resolve_action_complete.description: The value is true when action related to resolving an notification is successfully executed.

error_status.description: Error provided by the notification action.

is_ignored.description: Is the notification ignored by the plugin.

notification_target_uuid.description: The UUID of the notification target which contains the action to be performed for the notification.

v1NotificationPluginOperation.description:
- NOTIFICATION_PLUGIN_OPERATION_OPEN: Corresponds to creating a new notification.
 - NOTIFICATION_PLUGIN_OPERATION_UPDATE: Corresponds to updating an existing notification.
 - NOTIFICATION_PLUGIN_OPERATION_RESOLVE: Corresponds to resolving a notification.

project_uuid.description: The UUID of the project to which this notification belongs.

policy_uuid.description: The UUID of the policy to which this notification belongs.

notification_action_data.description:
A map of metadata related to the notification action, indexed by the notification target UUID.

It maintains the following information related to every notification target.

- Flags to determine if an action related to a notification target
  is successfully completed or not.  If not, the action
  is retried in subsequent attempts. Flags are maintained for
  actions related to OPEN/UPDATE/RESOLVE.
- Metadata specific to a notification target type. Some of
  the notification target types need to maintain state so that
  subsequent updates to the notifications can operate on the
  same entity created by the notification. For example, JIRA
  updates needs to be performed on the same JIRA issue and
  hence the need to store JIRA ID.

hashsum.description:
The SHA-256 hash sum of the notification payload.
The notification payload is a list of UUIDs (of say Findings/Metrics) and/or
an opaque blob. The list of UUIDs and the opaque data is used as input for the hashing function.

The hashsum is used to determine if the latest notification payload has changed
so as to update the notification target when there is a change.

last_seen_at.description: The time when the notification was last raised.

resolved_at.description: The time when the notification was marked resolved.

finding_details.description: The map of finding UUID to finding object.

uuid.description: The UUID of the notification target.

propagate.description: Indicates whether the object should be visible in the child namespaces or not.

v1NotificationTarget.description: Indicates actions taken when a corresponding notification is raised.

priority.description:
The priority of the NuGet package manager.
Priorities are relative. If multiple package managers
have the same priority, the order between them can be random.
Lower number means higher priority.

url.description: The URL of the registry that the NuGet package manager must use.

user.description: The username to use to access this repository.

password.description: An encrypted password that must be provided by the repository owner.

v1NugetSpec.description: NugetSpec represents a configuration for the NuGet package manager.

uuid.description: The UUID of the SBOM.

v1OSSDependencyRequest.description: Represents a request to create OSS dependencies in the system.

dependencies.description: The list of dependencies to be created as OSS dependencies.

uuid.description: The UUID of the onprem scheduler.

helm_values.description: Contains the base64 encoded helm values.yaml file.

scheduler_version.description: Contains the version of the scheduler.

uuid.description: The UUID of the resource.

v1Onboard.description: Onboard initiates the onboarding of a new tenant in the system.

invitation_uuid.description:
The UUID of an invitation.
In this case, a new tenant is not created, but the user joins
the tenant referred by the invitation.

v1Organization.description: The ingested information about an organization.

mfa_external.description: Multi-factor authentication is required for external contributors.

mfa_internal.description: Multi-factor authentication is required for internal contributors.

allow_public_repo_create.description: Allows members to create public repositories.

allow_private_repo_create.description: Allows members to create private repositories.

allow_internal_repo_create.description: Allows members to create internal repositories.

allow_private_repo_fork.description: Allows members to fork private repositories.

repo_get_permission.description:
Default permission required to run GET queries on repository.
Can be one of: "read", "write", "admin", "none".

repo_edit_permission.description:
Default permission required to execute EDIT commands on repository.
Can be one of: "read", "write", "admin", "none".

admins.description: List of all organization administrator usernames.

workflows_can_approve_prs.description: Allows workflows to approve pull request reviews.

default_workflow_permissions.description:
Default GITHUB_TOKEN permissions for workflows.
Can be one of: "read", "write".

v1OrganizationAccess.description: OrganizationAccess contains normalized organization access settings.

uuid.description: The UUID of an organization allowed workflows object.

v1OrganizationAllowedWorkflows.description: Represents the information of allowed workflows of an organization.

uuid.description: The UUID of an organization's block list.

v1OrganizationBlockList.description: Represents the information about the blocked user list of a platform organization.

uuid.description: The UUID of the CI/CD permissions object in a platform organization.

v1OrganizationCICDPermissions.description: Capture the permissions policy configuration for repositories.

v1OrganizationCustomPermissionList.description: Represents the ingested information about the custom fine grained permissions of a platform organization.

uuid.description: The UUID of a custom role list in an organization.

v1OrganizationCustomRoleList.description: OrganizationCustomRoleList represents the information about a platform organization's custom roles.

uuid.description: The UUID of an organizatio hook.

v1OrganizationHook.description: OrganizationHook represents the information of hooks created for an organization.

uuid.description: The UUID of the installation informatoin of a platform organization.

v1OrganizationInstallationList.description: OrganizationInstallationList represents the ingested information about the installation information of a platform organization.

uuid.description: The UUID of an invitation list of a platform organization.

v1OrganizationInvitationList.description: OrganizationInvitationList represents the ingested information about invitations of a platform organization.

uuid.description: The UUID of the member list of an organization.

v1OrganizationMemberList.description: OrganizationMemberList represents the ingested information about members of a platform organization.

uuid.description: The UUID of the outside collaborators list of a platform organization.

v1OrganizationOutsideCollaboratorList.description: OrganizationOutsideCollaboratorList represents the ingested information about outside collaborators of a platform organization.

uuid.description: The UUID of the runner group configuration of a platform organization.

v1OrganizationRunnerGroupConfig.description: OrganizationRunnerGroupConfig represents the ingested information about a self-hosted runner group configuration of a platform organization.

uuid.description: The UUID of the security managers list of a platform organization.

v1OrganizationSecurityManagerList.description: OrganizationSecurityManagerList represents the ingested information about security managers of a platform organization.

uuid.description: The UUID of the workflow permissions of an organization.

v1OrganizationWorkflowPermissions.description: OrganizationWorkflowPermissions represents the information of workflow permissions for an organization.

uuid.description: The UUID of a PR comment configuration.

findings_summary_template.description: The template for the PR comment for summary of findings.

uuid.description: The UUID of the package manager.

propagate.description: Propagate indicates that the object should be visible in child namespaces.

v1PackageManager.description:
PackageManager holds the data for the various package
managers of the enterprise. Mainly locations and
access credentials when needed to resolve/scan packages
from private package managers.
An enterprise can create one or more package managers
in a namespace. All the package managers of the given
namespace will be taken into account when resolving.

registry_url.description:
The URL to the registry of the package.
For example, for Maven central, https://repo.maven.apache.org/maven2.
For a local repository, https://myrepo.
When not provided, we assume the default public repository for each
package manager.

uuid.description: The UUID of the package version resource.

v1PackageVersion.description: PackageVersion represents a version of a package.

v1PackageVersionDependency.description: PackageVersionDependency is a dependency of a package version.

uuid.description: The UUID of the query and makes the object compatible with the system.

package_version_name.description: The fully qualified package name must be always provided.

requests.description: Caller can issue multiple requests (up to 32).

uuid.description: The UUID of the package version metadata.

v1PackageVersionMetadata.description: PackageVersionMetadata represents a metadata for a package version.

uuid.description: The UUID of the query and makes the object compatible with the system.

v1PackageVersionMetadataQuery.description:
PackageVersionMetadataQuery identifies the origin and metadata
of a package or package version from the OSS ecosystems.
The query accepts as input the name of the package with an optional
version and identifies whether the package can be found in the open
source ecosystem, the associated versions and release dates and
source code references.

package_name.description: The package name must be always provided.

version.description:
If a version is provided, the query returns the details
of the specific version (including source code reference and release date).
If the version is not provided, the query will return a list
of all versions known.

found.description: Indicates that details for this package version were found.

requests.description: Caller can issue multiple requests (up to 32).

last_updated.description: The last time that this entry was updated.

uuid.description: The UUID of the package version source metadata.

v1PackageVersionSourceMetadata.description:
PackageVersionSourceMetadata contains source code information for a PackageVersion.
For example, for the Java ecosystem this contains details such as checksum values of the artifact,
list of class files, and their corresponding checksums and import statements found.

artifact.description:
Name and checksum of the artifact for the package version, used to verify data integrity.
For the same package version, certain ecosystems may chose to publish multiple types of
artifact. For example, for Java we can have jar and war.

is_reconciled.description: True indicates the GAV package data has been reconciled to distinguish rebundled vs application code.

project_uuid.description: The UUID of the project to which this package version belongs.

release_timestamp.description:
The release timestamp corresponding to the time a particular package
version was released.

unresolved_dependencies.description:
The exact dependency declarations in the package manager descriptor file.
In Golang, this represents the list of dependencies in the go.mod.
In Java/Maven, this represents the list of dependencies in the pom.xml.

package_name.description:
The name of the package of this package version. It is
calculated automatically from the package version name.

relative_path.description:
Relative path of the package from where the package was discovered
relative to the workspace root.

call_graph_available.description: Set to true if a call graph was successfully created by the latest scan.

internal_reference_key.description: Unique key for the package generated by Endor Labs to simplify lookups.

host.description:
The host name of the registry or repository where the PHP packages are
hosted.

user.description: The user name to use to access the given host.

password.description: An encrypted password that must be provided by the repository owner.

v1PackagistSpec.description: Represents a configuration for the Packagist(PHP) package manager.

rules.title:
For message (or resource) repository, I want to provide permissions READ
only. This will create map["repository"] = []{READ}

except_resources.description: List of resources that should not be accounted in any * resource rules.

v1Permissions.description:
Holds a map associating each resource kind with the
 set of methods that are allowed for this kind.

 For example:

uuid.description: The UUID of a platform account.

v1PlatformAccount.description: PlatformAccount represents an account of a user to third-party site like GitHub/Gitlab or some package platform.

external_id.description: The ID of the platform account in the orginal platform.

name.description: The name of the platform account.

email.description: The email of the platform account.

create_time.description: Create time of the platform account.

update_time.description: Update time of the platform account.

uuid.description: The UUID of the platform organization.

v1PlatformOrganization.description: PlatformOrganization represents an organization on a third party site like Github/Gitlab or some package platform.

external_id.description: The ID of the platform organization in the orginal platform.

name.description: The name of the platform organization.

email.description: The email of the platform organization.

create_time.description: Create time of the platform organization.

update_time.description: Update time of the platform organization.

v1PlatformSource.description: Type of source control platform a resource was discovered on.

uuid.description: The UUID of the plugin binary.

version.description: The version of the plugin binary.

url.description: The URL from which the binary is retrieved.

published_date.description: The date when this binary was published.

unsupported.description: Unsupported identifies this binary as not officially supported by Endor Labs.

compatibility_matrix.description: Compatibility matrix containing the min/max supported values for runtime tools.

uuid.description: The UUID of the policy.

propagate.description: Whether the object should be visible in child namespaces or not.

v1Policy.description: Policy represents a policy in the system.

notification_target_uuids.description: The list of UUIDs of the notification targets that are configured for this policy.

bypass_exceptions.description: Set to true if exception policies should be ignored for findings matching this policy.

violating_resources.description:
Lists of UUIDs of resources that violate the policy,
if applicable, indexed by resource name.

allow.description:
True if input data is accepted by policy.
False if input data violates the expected state.

rule.description:
The policy rule in text format.

For example:

package policyexample

default allow = false

allow {
   data.resources.Project[_].meta.name = go-memdb"
}

project_selector.description:
The set of tags that projects must have for the policy to be executed against. If left empty, the policy
applies to all projects of the namespace. The items in the array
correspond to an OR operator. That is, any project with a tag that matches any
of the project_selector values will be matched.

project_exceptions.description:
The set of tags that projects that are matched
with are excluded from the policy. Allows exceptions. Exceptions
can be provided with or without selectors.

resource_kinds.description:
Identifies all resources that a policy requires.
For example, "Project", "Repository", "RepositoryVersion", "PackageVersion", "DependencyMetadata", "Metric", "LinterResult", "Finding", etc.
For the "Metric" resource you must also specify the corresponding parent resource kind.

disable.description: Disables a policy without deleting it.

query_statements.description:
The list of OPA query statements that must be executed to evaluate the policy.
For example, [ "data.packagename.allow" ], [opulated from template if applicable.

template_uuid.description: Indicates that the policy is created from a template.

template_values.description: The map of key-value pairs for parameters used to customize the template.

template_parameters.description: Template parameter metadata for this template version.

group_by_fields.description:
Custom fields to group policy output by. This may be a
subset of all custom keys in the policy output. You do not need to
add all custom fields here, just the ones you want to be used to
group the matches by. The corresponding values
are always treated as a single string.

uuid.description: The UUID of the policy template.

propagate.description: Whether the object should be visible in child namespaces or not.

v1PolicyTemplate.description:
PolicyTemplate represents a policy system in the system. Policy
templates can be used to create policies from these templates.

rule.description:
The policy template rule in text format.

For example:

package policyexample

default allow = false

allow {
   input.Value1 = input.Value2
}

template_parameters.description:
The list of values to customize the template. In the example above,
Value1 and Value2.

resource_kinds.description:
The additional resources that a policy requires.
By default, policies are executed against Project, Repository,
RepositoryVersion, PackageVersion, Metric and Finding. If a policy
must include additional objects they must be defined here.

query_statements.description:
The list of OPA query statements that must be executed to evaluate the resulting policy.
For example, [ "data.packagename.allow" ].

default_enabled.description:
True if generated out-of-the-box policy is enabled by default
(unless Policy.AutoEnable is explicitly disabled in SystemConfig).
Default user finding policies are also generated for new tenants
if this flag is set to true.

group_by_fields.description:
Custom fields to group policy output by. This may be a subset of all
custom keys in the policy output. You do not need to add all custom fields here.
You can add just the custom fileds you want to be used to group the matches by.
The corresponding values are always treated as a single string.

release_notes.description: Release notes indexed by template version.

deprecated.description:
Set to True if the policy template has been deprecated.
Policies created from deprecated templates are never deleted,
but the template is no longer visible in the UI.

uuid.description: The UUID of the resource.

v1PolicyValidation.description: PolicyValidation represents a policy validation request and response.

project_uuid.description: The UUID of project to load data from [optional].

query_statements.description:
List of OPA query statements that must be executed to
evaluate the policy (for example, "data.packagename.allow").

rule.description: Policy Rego rule in text format.

template_values.description: The map of key-value pairs for parameters used to customize the policy Rego rule.

template_uuid.description: The UUID of policy template from which the policy will be created.

project_selector.title:
The set of tags that projects must have for the policy to be executed against. If left empty, the policy
applies to all projects of the namespace. The items in the array
correspond to an OR operator. (ie any project with a tag that matches any
of the project_selector values will be matched.)

project_exceptions.description:
The set of tags that projects that are matched
with are excluded from the policy. Allows exceptions. Exceptions
can be provided with or without selectors.

resource_kinds.description:
Identifies all resources that a policy requires.
For example, "Project", "Repository", "RepositoryVersion", "PackageVersion", "DependencyMetadata", "Metric", "LinterResult", "Finding", etc.
For the "Metric" resource you must also specify the corresponding parent resource kind.

target_kind.description:
Repository, RepositoryVersion, or PackageVersion.
This is usually the same as the finding parent kind.
Only applicable to SYSTEM and USER_FINDING policies.

disable_preview.description: Set to true to validate the policy without project data.

v1PolicyValidationRequest.description:
PolicyValidationRequest represents a policy validation request.
If no project is specified the policy is validated against all projects.

v1PrecomputedState.description:
PrecomputedState represents the state of precomputed operations.

 - PRECOMPUTED_STATE_UNSPECIFIED: UNSPECIFIED indicates that the precomputed state is not set or unknown.
 - PRECOMPUTED_STATE_SUCCESS: SUCCESS indicates that the precomputed operation completed successfully.
 - PRECOMPUTED_STATE_FAILURE: FAILURE indicates that the precomputed operation failed.

v1PrioritizationConfig.description: Configuration of the factors that control the prioritization of artifacts.

name.description: External name for the factor.

scan_time.description:
Last time a project was ingested.

Format: 2017-01-15T01:30:15.01Z
RFC 3339: https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc3339.txt

analytic_time.description:
Last time a project was analyzed.

Format: 2017-01-15T01:30:15.01Z
RFC 3339: https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc3339.txt.

queue_time.description:
Last time a project was queued.

Format: 2017-01-15T01:30:15.01Z
RFC 3339: https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc3339.txt.

disable_automated_scan.description:
This is a private package and it must not be scanned by the background
scheduler. It will be scanned by an endorctl client instead. Default:
false.

full_history_scan_time.description:
Last time a project was scanned with deep secrets scanning
(--git-logs).

Format: 2017-01-15T01:30:15.01Z
RFC 3339: https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc3339.txt.

uuid.description: The unique identifier of the project.

v1Project.description:
Logical root object that serves as the basis for organizing and managing all
information related to a repository.

A project corresponds to a repository.

internal_reference_key.description: Unique key for the project generated by Endor Labs to simplify lookups.

ingestion_token.description: Third-party API authentication token to read project related information.

toolchain_profile_uuid.description: The toolchain required to build and scan the project.

automated_scan_enabled.description:
Set to true if automated scans are enabled for the project.
This only applies to projects scanned by the GitHub App.
It does not prevent the daily analytics-only scans from running.

last_scanned.description:
The last time the project was scanned by the Endor Labs scheduler.
This is the same as the update time of this object.

uuid.description: UUID is a universally unique identifier for the provisioning result.

v1ProvisioningResult.description:
ProvisioningResult represents the versions that are maintained
by Endor Labs.

os.description: The OS where the provisioning occurred.

arch.description: The OS architecture where the provisioning occurred.

project_url.description: Repository URL of the project for which the scan is run.

v1ProvisioningResultSpec.description: Spec is the internal specification of the object.

file_changes.description:
Comprehensive information about all file changes in the pull request.
The map key is the file path.

security_metrics.title:
Security review findings and metrics
Detailed security metrics and findings

v1PullRequestSecurityReviewResultInfo.description:
PullRequestSecurityReviewResultInfo represents the comprehensive results of an automated security review
performed on a pull request. It includes detailed information about code changes, security metrics,
and relevant code snippets that may have security implications.

priority.description:
The priority of the Python package manager.
Priorities are relative. If multiple package managers
have the same priority, the order between them can be random.
Lower number means higher priority.

url.description: The URL of the registry that the Python package manager must use.

user.description: The user name to use to access this repository.

password.description: An encrypted password that must be provided by the repository owner.

v1PypiSpec.description: PypiSpec represents a configuration for the Python package manager.

pip_packages.description: the key is the package name to install through pip.

uuid.description: The UUID of the resource.

v1Query.description: Query implements the metric query requests.

v1QueryMalware.description:
QueryMalware is used to do query request on malware that are
not supported by ListMalware and the filter. For instance, you must
use this method to get a list of malware based on a version.

purls.description:
a list of PURLs to lookup.
Using a PURL for the lookup allows the caller to provide additional
metadata.

mask.description:
Mask specifies the list of fields to return with a request.
If no mask is given, all fields will be returned by the API.

connect_to.description: The child field to connect to. If empty, it is parent_uuid.

connect_from.description: The parent field from which we are connecting. If emtpy, it is UUID.

connect_operator.description:
The operation that should be used to connect
parent and child. If empty it is the equal operator. Only
supporting scalar value operators at this time.

uuid.description: The UUID of the resource.

query_response.description: Aggregates the results of the query.

v1QuerySimilarPackages.description: QuerySimilarPackages implements the similar packages query requests.

name.description: The name of the package for which similar packaged are being queried.

v1QueryVulnerability.description:
QueryVulnerability is used to do query request on vulnerabilities that are
not supported by ListVulnerabilities and the filter. For instance, you must
use this method to get a list of vulnerabilities based on a version.

purls.description:
a list of PURLs to lookup.
Using a PURL for the lookup allows the caller to provide additional
metadata.

mask.description:
Mask specifies the list of fields to return with a request.
If no mask is given, all fields will be returned by the API.

raw_response.description: Raw response received from the query.

exists.description: Extracted boolean whether or not specified field in prompt exists.

answer.description: Extracted string answer from the raw response.

evidence.description: Extracted evidence supporting answer from the raw response.

upgrade_if_better_scores.description: Whether to consider a version upgrade just to improve the Endor scores or not.

score_improvement_threshold.description: Score improvement to consider an upgrade.

prefer_minor_version_upgrades.description: Give higher priority to upgrades to versions with the same major version since these are more likely to be compatible.

upgrade_if_operational_findings.description: Whether to consider a version upgrade if the current version has only operational findings or not.

upgrade_if_vulnerability_findings.description: Whether to consider a version upgrade if the current version has vulnerability findings or not.

no_upgrade_package_set.description: These packages can not be upgraded and will be skipped.

use_cia.description: Whether using change impact analysis or not.

skip_test.description: If true, ignores all the direct dependencies that are marked as TEST/BUILD.

top_only.description: Only show the top N dependency upgrades.

target_dependency.description: Only analyze this direct dependency and ignore the others.

root_package_version.description: The project package that imports this dependency.

root_pkg_version_uuid.description: The UUID of the root package version.

from_version.description: The current version of the dependency.

from_pkg_version_uuid.description: The UUID of the package version of the current dependency version.

to_version.description: The version to which the package is to be upgraded.

to_pkg_version_uuid.description: The UUID of the package version of the dependency version to which we upgrade.

current_conflicts.description: The count of conflicts with even the current version.

conflicts.description: The number of conflicts between the dependencies of the two versions.

conflicts_map.description: The list of conflicting direct dependency packages.

minor_conflicts.description: The number of minor (same major version) conflicts between the dependencies of the two versions.

minor_conflicts_map.description: The list of minor (same major version) conflicts.

deps_added.description: The number of new dependencies added in the upgrade.

deps_removed.description: The number of dependencies removed in the upgrade.

cur_dep_count.description: The number of dependencies in the current version.

update_dep_count.description: The number of dependencies in the upgrade.

score.description: The internal score for this upgrade.

worth_it.description: Whether the upgrade worth it or not.

is_latest.description: Whether this an upgrade to the latest version or not.

is_best.description: Whether this the best upgrade according to our analysis or not.

endor_scores_improvement.title: The breakdown of the Endor score improvements in the upgrade

package_count.description: The number of packages affected by the upgrade.

cia_status.description: The status of the CIA analysis, including errors.

from_version_publish_time.description: Publish time of the from version.

to_version_publish_time.description: Publish time of the to version.

direct_dependency_manifest_files.description: Manifest file information for the direct dependency being upgraded.

total_findings_fixed.description: Count of findings (all types and severity levels) fixed by the upgrade.

total_findings_introduced.description: Count of findings (all types and severity levels) introduced by the upgrade.

is_endor_patch.description: True if this is an Endor Labs Assured package version.

project_uuid.description: Project UUID.

package_manager_version_count.description: The number of versions for direct dependencies that we found in the package manager.

database_version_count.description: The number of versions for direct dependencies we found in our database.

missing_version_count.description: The number of direct dependency versions we miss from our database.

incomplete_releases.description: Releases not considered because of lack of information.

v1RemediationParameters.description: RemediationParameters holds the settings for remediation actions.

uuid.description: The UUID of the source control repository.

v1Repository.description:
Repository represents the ingested information about a source control repository.
A repository may refer to any type of source control repository, such as GitLab or GitHub.

allow_merge_commit.description: Repository allows merging pull requests with a merge commit.

allow_auto_merge.description: Repository allows auto-merge on pull requests.

require_web_commit_signoff.description: Require developers to sign off on commits made through a web interface.

advanced_security_enabled.description: Advanced security is automatically enabled for new repositories.

admins.description: List of all repository administrator usernames.

default_branch_protection_status.description: Branch protection status of default branch.

pushed_at.description: Time of the most recent commit to the repository.

v1RepositoryBranchProtection.description: Branch protection information of a single branch.

branch_name.description: The name of the branch to which the branch protections applies.

uuid.description: The UUID of the repository check run.

v1RepositoryCheckRun.description: RepositoryCheckRun represents the information about a repository check run.

commit_sha.description: The SHA to which the check run is attached.

uuid.description: The UUID of the repository's codeowners file information.

v1RepositoryCodeownersFile.description:
The information about a repository's codeowners file.
It does not contain the actual file contents.

uuid.description: The UUID fir repository's collaborators information.

v1RepositoryCollaborators.description: The ingested information about a repository's collaborators.

is_admin.description: Whether the collaborator has admin permissions or not.

uuid.description: The UUID of the repository commit.

v1RepositoryCommit.description: Represents the ingested information about a repository commit.

tree_hash.description: The hash of the root tree of the commit.

parent_hashes.description: The hashes of the parent commits of the commit.

uuid.description: The UUID of the repository's deployment environment configuration.

v1RepositoryDeploymentEnvConfig.description:
The ingested information about a repository's deployment environment configurations.
https://docs.github.com/en/rest/deployments/environments?apiVersion=2022-11-28#list-environments.

uuid.description: The UUID of the for the repository hook.

v1RepositoryHook.description: The information about hooks created for a repository.

uuid.description: The UUID of a repository issue.

v1RepositoryIssue.description: The ingested information about a repository issue.

external_id.description: The ID of the object in the platform from which the issue was ingested.

create_time.description: Create time of the repository issue in the platform.

update_time.description: Update time of the repository issue in the platform.

close_time.description: Close time of the repository issue in the platform.

name.description: Name of the source control repository software license.

A commit hash contains all commits branches that were already ingested.

For example: with branch A, branch B and branch C, where branch A and branch B were

target.description:
The target object (path or package) where the failure happened. For package
scans it is the package. For the workspace scans it is the target path.

description.description:
A description of the error. Plugins should use proper descriptions that
will be helpful to users or operations.

operation.description:
The operation during which encountered the error.
A Go plugin can return as operation reading go.mod file,
a Java plugin can be reading a pom.xml. Every plugin is
doing different operations and it should identify here
the operation that failed. This again will help with debugging.

error_analysis.description:
The analysis of the error based on the error parsing rules
that can provide additional context on possible fixes.

target_files.description: The content of manifest files or the list of files based on the operation.

v1ResolutionStatus.description:
ResolutionStatus is the response status that indicates if the operation
succeeded. The response status will be stored with the
results. The caller will continue with subsequent requests
and the server can continue with additional packages.

v1ScanState.description:
Scan state for a project or package version.

 - SCAN_STATE_NOT_PROCESSED: Object has not been processed by the system yet.
Set when the project or package is created for the first time.
A new project or package version is automatically placed in this state.
 - SCAN_STATE_IDLE: Object has been scanned at least once.
 - SCAN_STATE_INGESTING: Object is being scanned.
 - SCAN_STATE_ANALYTIC: Object is being analyzed.
 - SCAN_STATE_UNREACHABLE: Object cannot be ingested because it is not reachable from the scheduler.
 - SCAN_STATE_REQUEST_FULL_RESCAN: Object is marked for a complete rescan.
This only applies to OSS projects.
 - SCAN_STATE_REQUEST_INCREMENTAL_RESCAN: Object is marked for an incremental rescan, where only new packages
discovered in the scan are added. Indicates that this project should be
rescanned with a higher priority. Often because it is used by a customer
and we need any new packages to be discovered and scanned with higher
priority. If the project is put in this state, we will scan any new
packages at high priority but we will not re-scan older packages.
 - SCAN_STATE_QUEUED: Object is queued to be scanned.
 - SCAN_STATE_QUARANTINED: Scan scheduling was unsuccessful due to a system errors. Object is
quarantined from additional scheduling. Potentially object is an
orphan or similar situations.

uuid.description: The unique identifier of the scan workflow.

v1ScanWorkflow.description: ScanWorkflow corresponds to a workflow of scan steps.

uuid.description: The unique identifier of the scan workflow result.

v1ScanWorkflowResult.description: ScanWorkflowResult corresponds to a workflow scan result.

execution_id.description: The execution ID for all scans.

uuid.description: The UUID of a scope policy.

v1ScopePolicy.description:
ScopePolicy represents a scope policy in the system. Scope policies allow users
to select for a set of projects (or namespaces) as defined by the project selectors.
It is the set of policies that are applied to these projects as defined by a policy selector.

project_namespace_selector.description:
The list of namespaces for which this scope rule will apply.
For example, defining a namespace "tenant" will mean that the rule applies for all
projects in the namespace.

project_tag_selector.description:
The set of tags that projects must have for the scope policy to be executed against.
If left empty, the scope policy applies to all projects of the namespace. The items in the array
correspond to an OR operator. That is, any project with a tag that matches any
of the project_selector values will be matched.

excluded_project_tag_selector.description: The set of tags that projects must not have for the scope policy to be executed against.

policy_namespace_selector.description:
Selects all policies in the namespace to be applied.
If the policy_namespace_selector is defined, the policy_tag_selector cannot be defined at the same time.
For example, defining a policy_namespace_selector of "tenant" will indicate that all policies
in the tenant must be applied to the projects defined with the above selectors.

policy_tag_selector.description: The set of tags that policies must match to be applied to a project.

v1ScoreCard.description: A scorecard contains a total score per ScoreCategory.

v1ScoreCategory.description:
The types of scores that Endor Labs tracks.

 - SCORE_CATEGORY_POPULARITY: Tracks a project's popularity.
 - SCORE_CATEGORY_ACTIVITY: Tracks the amount of activity around a project.
 - SCORE_CATEGORY_BEST_PRACTICES: Tracks how much a project follows various development best practices.
 - SCORE_CATEGORY_SUSPICIOUS_ACTIVITY: Tracks suspicious activity on a project.
 - SCORE_CATEGORY_DEPENDENCIES: Tracks the dependencies of a project.
 - SCORE_CATEGORY_SECURITY: Tracks security aspects of a project.
 - SCORE_CATEGORY_CODE_QUALITY: Tracks the code quality of a project.
 - SCORE_CATEGORY_OPERATIONAL_RISK: Tracks the operationa risk of a project.

description.description: Text description of the factor and its meaning.

name.description: An external name for the factor.

evidence.description: The details of what the factor was raised.

id.description: Unique ID to identify the factor.

score_float.description: The float value of the score for the factor.

v1ScoreFactor.description:
A score factor for an object. Each score factor
describes a piece of information used while computing the overall score.
Each factor is associated with a specific category. Score factors can have a
positive impact on the repository score and then they have a positive score, or can
have a negative impact on the overall score and then they have a positive score.
The description captures additional information for the particular factor.
In general there are many (tens or hundreds) of such score factors split across the ScoreCategory categories.

Postive score factor example:

 Category: SCORE_CATEGORY_BEST_PRACTICES

 Score: 2

 Description: The repository is enforcing signed commits.

Negative score factor example:

Category: SCORE_CATEGORY_VULNERABILITIES

Score: -1

Description: the repository has unresolved issues related to vulnerabilities that have been open for more than 3 months.

v1ScoreFactorList.description: A list of score factors that are directly exported by the related anlaytics.

A segment index request allows the caller to provide a set of code

embeddings store. Embeddings are only created. They are only read through the
SegmentQueryRequest. The request can include multiple segments to allow
parallel execution.

v1SemgrepRuleSpecSeverityLevel.description:
The severity level of the rule. This is computed using the values of the confidence and
the impact from the rule itself. If those values are not defined, the severity level is
set to LOW.

v1SemgrepTriggerPattern.description: A pattern that triggered the vulnerability scan.

description.description: The reasoning behind this service request.

resource.description: The resource name on which the requested action needs to be performed.

target_namespace.description: The resource namespace on which operations needs to be performed.

approvers.description:
The list of approver email IDs to which this service request
notifications will be sent.

error_message.description: Error message when the request fails.

uuid.description: The UUID of the request.

v1ServiceToken.description: ServiceToken represents a request for a service token.

audience.description: The audience for the service token.

expiration_time.description:
expiration_time for the token that should before the
expiration time of the authentication token.

service_token.description: service_token is the token that is generated.

rfilename.description: The filename within a specific model.

v1SimpleContainerImage.description:
SimpleContainerImage describes the structure of a basic container image signature payload, as defined at:
https://github.com/containers/image/blob/main/docs/containers-signature.5.md#json-data-format.

We don't really need to use these definitions since they are only kept in our database and will
not be visible externally. However, if we ever decide to save our signatures as part of the OCI
container registries or even be asked to "export" our data for customers that perhaps want to
migrate to cosign or notary, then this will make this effort more seamless because it's the
standard format used.

open_action_template.description: The template to be used for OPEN action for slack plugin.

update_action_template.description: The template to be used for UPDATE action for slack plugin.

v1StringList.description: Contains a list of string values.

uuid.description: The uuid of the resource.

propagate.description: Indicates that the object should be visible in the child namespaces.

v1SupportAccessRequest.description:
Implements the API to grant access to support for a given namespace. It will
create an authorization policy that allows access to the support team.

expiration_time.description: Expiration time of the support access.

uuid.description: UUID is a universally unique identifier for the tool chain profile.

v1SupportedToolChainProfile.description:
SupportedToolChainProfile represents the list of tool chains that are maintained
by Endor Labs.

v1SupportedToolChainProfileSpec.description: Spec is the internal specification of the object.

v1SupportedToolChainVersionSpec.description: Spec is the internal specification of the object.

priority.description:
The priority of the Swift package manager.
Priorities are relative. If multiple package managers
have the same priority, the order between them can be random.
Lower number means higher priority.

url.description: The URL of the registry that the Swift package manager must use.

token.description: Access token to authenticate to the registry.

v1SwiftSpec.description: Represents a configuration for the Swift package manager.

uuid.description: The UUID of the system configuration.

propagate.description: Indicates that the object should be visible in child namespaces.

v1SystemConfig.description: SystemConfig represents the system configuration for a tenant.

v1SystemEvaluationMethodDefinition.description:
SystemEvaluationMethodDefinition is the type of evaluation method implemented
by the system.

 - SYSTEM_EVALUATION_METHOD_DEFINITION_VULNERABILITIES: VULNERABILITIES calculates vulnerability related findings.
 - SYSTEM_EVALUATION_METHOD_DEFINITION_SCORES: SCORES calculates score related findings.
 - SYSTEM_EVALUATION_METHOD_DEFINITION_CONDITIONS: CONDITIONS calculates findings related to specific conditions.
 - SYSTEM_EVALUATION_METHOD_DEFINITION_POLICIES: POLICIES evaluates methods based on user defined policies.
 - SYSTEM_EVALUATION_METHOD_DEFINITION_TYPOSQUATTING: TYPOSQUATTING calculates the findings related to typosquatted packages.
 - SYSTEM_EVALUATION_METHOD_DEFINITION_CIS: CIS calculates the findings related to CIS benchmark requirements.
 - SYSTEM_EVALUATION_METHOD_DEFINITION_MALWARE: MALWARE calculates the findings related to malware.
 - SYSTEM_EVALUATION_METHOD_DEFINITION_SECURITY_REVIEW: SECURITY REVIEW calculates security review related findings.

v1SystemRole.description:
Captures the default system roles for permissions.

 - SYSTEM_ROLE_ADMIN: ADMIN has read/write access to all objects in the tenant.
 - SYSTEM_ROLE_READ_ONLY: READ_ONLY only can read all data, but perform no updates.
 - SYSTEM_ROLE_POLICY_EDITOR: Policy editor has read access on all objects, and the ability to create and
manage policies.
 - SYSTEM_ROLE_CODE_SCANNER: CODE_SCANNER has all permissions to run a scan and view the results.
 - SYSTEM_ROLE_ENDOR_PATCHING: ENDOR_PATCHING has permissions to use Endor Lab's factory.
 - SYSTEM_ROLE_SYNC_ORG: SYNC_ORG has permissions to perform a sync-org scan on all supported
platforms (GitHub, Azure, GitLab etc).
 - SYSTEM_ROLE_ONPREM_SCHEDULER: ONPREM_SCHEDULER has permissions to perform sync-org, scans and manage
onprem scheduler related objects.

columns.description: The number of columns in the table.

column_ids.description:
A map from a column name to an ID. The IDs in the rows of the
table are used to avoid repeating the column name.

column_types.description: A map from a column ID to a type. This will be used to read the values of the columns in each row.

v1TableHeader.description:
Contains information for the table.
It is repeated in each TableRowGroup even though it contains the same information.

v1TableRow.description: A table row contains a map with the values for each column.

uuid.description: The UUID of the table row group.

v1TableRowGroup.description:
TableRowGroup in the main object that contains an ordered list of TableRows
and TableDescription with general information about the table.

project_uuid.description: The UUID of the project that to which the TableRowGroup is related.

sequence.description: The sequence number indicates the order of this group among all the groups in the table.

name.description: Target name. Example: linux, dotnet, x86_64.

version.description:
Target version, for example, 6.0.0 for the dotnet framework
version 6.0.0. If there is no version specified for a target then that
means that a dependency is applicable for all versions of that target.
This field is optional because few targets, such as CPU_ARCH,
do not have a version.

exclude.description: Set to true if a dependency is excluded from the target.

uuid.description: The UUID of the telemetry.

v1Telemetry.description: Telemetry represents the Prometheus metrics obtained from endorctl processes.

metric_families.description: An array of metric families sent to the server.

execution_id.description: The UUID for the endorctl run.

caller_prefix.description: Identifies where the metrics come from.

uuid.description: The UUID of a tenant.

v1Tenant.description:
Tenant represents a tenant in the system.

Mostly includes tenant configuration parameters.

namespace.description:
Namespaces are a way to organize organizational units into virtual
groupings of resources. Namespaces must be a fully qualified name,
for example, the child namespace of namespace "endor.prod" called "app"
is called "endor.prod.app".

v1TenantMeta.description: Tenant related data for the tenant containing the resource.

street.description: The street address of a user.

city.description: The city of a users address.

state.description: The state of a users address.

organizational_entity_name.title:
The name of the organization that they will use as supplier for the SBOM.
although this is not a required field this will be validated when trying to export an SBOM.bool

organizational_contacts.description: The list of contact that we can put as the contact in the SBOM export supplier information.

organization_urls.description: The list of URLs which are associated with the given organization which can be included in the supplier SBOM information.

timestamp.description:
Timestamp of the slot. The resolution of this timestamp
depends on the time resolution we are tracking, so for
daily resolution will be the 12:00am of the day.

count.description: The count of activity in the slot.

tag_activity.description:
The breakdown of activity per tag. When we track an activity,
it can be associated with a user specified tag (for example, "Open" for a PR).
So here we get the per-tag breakdown.

v1TimeSlot.description:
Structure that contains activity information for a given slot of time.
Each slot could  capture activity for one day, month or year, depending on the resolution used.

v1TimeTracker.description:
Tge sequence of TimeSlots for each time resolution we track.
We can track multiple resolutions, and some resolutions may be empty.

v1ToolChainProfile.description:
ToolChainProfile represents the list of tool chains that need to be
installed.

name.description: name of the tool chain.

relative_tool_chain_path.description: relative path of the tool chain in tar ball.

urls.description: http mirros from where the tool can be downloaded.

sha256_sum.description: SHA256 Digest of the tool that will be downloaded.

v1ToolChainVersion.description: ToolChainVersion defines the tool chain's source from where it can be installed.

environment_variables.description: environment_variables are the env variables to be set during toolchain installation.

uuid.description: The UUID of the tool pattern.

propagate.description: Indicates that the object should be visible in child namespaces.

v1ToolPattern.description: ToolPattern represents the search pattern data for one tool.

categories.description:
List of control function categories (based on static name to category mapping).
Examples: Build, CI/CD Pipeline, Code Coverage, Container Orchestration, Container Registry, Dependency Mgmt, Documentation, IaC, License Mgmt, Linters, Monitoring, Package Mgmt, Publishers, SBOM, SCA, Secrets, Security, Signing and Verification, Testing, VMs, Vulnerability Mgmt.

file_patterns.description:
List of search patterns that are applied file/path names (filename_patterns) and, if present, their content (line_patterns).
Examples:
A file name match for "Dockerfile$" identifies Docker.
A match for line_pattern "\[tool\.black\]" inside a file with a filename_pattern match for "pyproject.toml$" idenfies Python Black.

cmd_patterns.description:
List of search patterns that are applied to the content of all CI/CD pipeline definition files.
Example: "endorctl scan" identifies Endor Labs usage inside tool configuration files that contain shell commands.

tool_patterns.description:
Map of search patterns that are applied to the content of specific tool definition files, indexed by tool name.
Example: ".*uses: sonarsource/sonarcloud-github-action@.*" identifies sonarcloud inside a GitHub Actions file, so it is listed under "ghactions" here.
The index string must match the name of the other tool in order for the infrastructure to cross-reference them.

webhook_patterns.description:
List of search patterns that are applied to webhook payload URLs.
Example: https://coveralls.io/webhook.

badge_patterns.description:
List of badge URL patterns.
Example: codecov.io.

display_name.description:
Display name if different from tool name.
Example: "GitHub Actions" might be the display name for "ghactions".

v1ToolPatternSpec.description: The internal specification of the object.

uuid.description: The UUID of the UI telemetry.

v1UITelemetry.description: UITelemetry represents events observered from the Endor UI.

user_id.description: The user to which these events belong.

session_id.description: The user session to which these events belong.

events.description: A batched list of events observed from the UI.

name.description:
Name of the unsupported project, which can be an unsupported clone URL or
a package name.

v1UnsupportedSpec.description: A project with a source code type that is not supported or not known.

update_mask.description: Fields to update. Defaults to all fields.

force.description:
Force will force the update of the resource if any
checks fail.

v1UpdateRequest.description: Message used for all update requests.

uuid.description: The UUID of a user.

v1User.description:
User represents a user in the system.

Mostly includes configuration parameters that are user wise.

v1UserInfoResponse.description: UserInfoResponse returns information about a user and their authentication token.

user_name.title:
Username of a user.
Usernames are automatically derived from the token.
Depending on the identity source it can be the
nickname (GitHub) or email (Google and others)

first_name.description: First name of the user as identified. It can be changed.

last_name.description: Last name of the user. It can be changed.

email.description: Email of the user. It can be changed.

last_login_time.description:
Last login time keeps a record of the last time
that the user logged in.

event_tracking.description: Lists of user events indexed by event type.

token_hash.description:
The hash of the last token issued to the user.
During authentication we make sure that this hash matches
the token provided.

groups.description:
Groups that the user is a member of, as identified by the
claims returned by the identity provider.

uuid.description: The UUID of the user telemetry.

v1UserTelemetry.description: UserTelemetry represents user-specific events observered from the Endor Labs UI.

user_id.description: The user to which these events belong.

session_id.description: The user session to which these events belong.

event_store.description: Lists of user events indexed by event type.

key.description: The unique identifier of the API key.

secret.description: The secret associated with the provided API Key.

valid.description: Valid returns if the API key and secret pair are valid.

v1ValidationStatus.description:
Status of secret validation.

 - VALIDATION_STATUS_UNSPECIFIED: No validator.
 - VALIDATION_STATUS_FAILURE: Failed to validate secret.
 - VALIDATION_STATUS_SECRET_IS_VALID: Secret is valid.
 - VALIDATION_STATUS_SECRET_IS_INVALID: Secret is invalid

vulnerable_component_resource_id.description: The Resource ID of the vulnerable component created in Vanta.

package_vulnerability_resource_id.description: The Resource ID of the package vulnerability created in Vanta.

sca_resource_id.description: The Resource ID of the static code analysis vulnerabilities created in Vanta.

vector_store_uuid.description: vector_store_uuid is the ID of the vector store to upload data.

documents.description: data is the data to upload to the vector store.

vector_store_uuid.description: vector_store_uuid is the ID of the vector store to upload data.

query.description: query is the actual query string.

matches.description: data is the data to upload to the vector store.

dimensions.description: Dimensions is the number of dimensions to be used.

sha.description:
SHA of the source control version.
Because the SHA might not be possible to resolved this field is optional.

ref.description:
Resolved ref of the source control version.
Can be a tag, a branch or a SHA.

uuid.description: The UUID of the version diff.
The UUID of the resource.

v1VersionUpgrade.description:
VersionUpgrade contains all information about a possible version upgrade of a dependency package.
This upgrade object can be under a project or a package version object.

project_uuid.description: The UUID of the project to which this version upgrade relates.

name.description: The name of the project or package version for this record.

uuid.description: The UUID of a vulnerability.

v1Vuln.description: Vuln represents an Endor Labs vulnerability in the system.

deepdive.description:
Indicates whether the research team performed full analysis on multiple
artifact_ids involved in CVE.

disputed.description:
Indicates whether the research team considers a CVE should be disputed
based on analysis.

additional_notes.description: Notes by the person that processed the CVE.

published.description: The RFC3339 timestamp indicating when this entry was published.

modified.description: The RFC3339 timestamp indicating when this entry was last modified.

withdrawn.description:
Optional. The RFC3339 timestamp indicating when this entry is considered
to be withdrawn.

aliases.description: Optional. IDs for the same vulnerability in other databases.

related.description:
Optional. List of IDs of closely related vulnerabilities, such as the
same problem in alternate ecosystems.

database_specific.description:
Optional. JSON object holding additional information about the
vulnerability as defined by the database for which the record applies.

malicious.description: Indicates whether this item is classified as malicious or not.

additional_endor_notes.description: Notes from the Endor Labs analysis of the vulnerability.

vulnerabilities.description: A list of all the vulnerabilities found affecting this package or package version.

v1VulnerabilityStats.description: VulnerabilityFactors message includes all vulnerability related data.
